The Elomin first denied the existence of the Eloms, and then later denied they were sentient.
variants.notes: Collapsed from the former separate Elomite (Variant) species row.

name: Elomin

structuralTraits.name: Frail Build
structuralTraits.description: An Elomin takes a -2 species penalty to Fortitude Defense.

structuralTraits.name: Technical Savant
structuralTraits.description: An Elomin gains a +2 species bonus on Mechanics checks.

description: Culturally, the Elomin try to discover or create order in all things. They view the universe as a logical, organized puzzle, and endeavor to find the pieces and bring them to their logical places. When confronted with something that doesn't fit into the pattern they have defined, they may try to redefine the pattern, but are just as likely to attempt to ignore the discordant details. Such was the case when the Elomin first discovered the existence of the underground-dwelling Eloms, another sentient Species native to their world. The Elomin first denied the existence of the Eloms, and then later denied they were sentient.

canonicalStats.abilityText: All Elomin receive a +2 bonus to their Intelligence, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Charisma. Elomin have trouble relating to other Species, but they are exceptionally good at a number of intellectual pursuits.

canonicalTraits.name: Driven
canonicalTraits.description: Elomin gain a +5 Species bonus to their Will Defense against Mind-Affecting effects.

canonicalTraits.name: Impose Order
canonicalTraits.description: Elomin are exceptionally focused on order, and strive to ensure that their chaotic companions are in the place where they are supposed to be. Once per encounter, an Elomin can spend a Swift Action to allow each ally within 12 squares to move up to half that ally's Speed as a Reaction. This movement does not provoke Attacks of Opportunity.

name: Ewok

structuralTraits.name: Small Size
structuralTraits.description: As a Small creature, an Ewok gains a +5 species bonus to Stealth checks and a +1 species bonus to Reflex Defense, but takes a -5 penalty on grapple checks.

structuralTraits.name: Primitive Weapons
structuralTraits.description: An Ewok gains proficiency with primitive weapons.

description: Intelligent omnivores native to one of the moons orbiting Endor, Ewoks are almost unknown prior to the battle of Endor. Ewoks live in tree-dwelling tribes with gender-based division of labor; males hunt, forage, and make weapons, while females raise young and handle other domestic tasks. Ewok culture revolves around complex animistic beliefs involving the giant trees of the forest moon. Although technologically primitive, Ewoks are clever, inquisitive, and inventive. Skittish and wary when first introduced to machines, their curiosity soon overcomes fear.

canonicalStats.abilityText: All Ewoks receive a +2 bonus to their Dexterity, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Strength. Ewoks are very agile but somewhat weak.

canonicalTraits.name: Conditional Bonus Feat
canonicalTraits.description: Ewoks are skilled foragers. An Ewok with Survival as a Trained Skill gains Skill Focus (Survival) as a bonus Feat.

canonicalTraits.name: Primitive
canonicalTraits.description: Ewoks do not gain Weapon Proficiency (Heavy Weapons), Weapon Proficiency (Pistols), or Weapon Proficiency (Rifles) as starting Feats at first level, even if their Class normally grants them.

canonicalTraits.name: Scent
canonicalTraits.description: Ewoks have a keen sense of smell. At close range (within 10 squares), Ewoks ignore Concealment and Cover for purposes of Perception checks, and take no penalty from poor visibility while Tracking.

canonicalTraits.name: Sneaky
canonicalTraits.description: An Ewok may choose to reroll any Stealth check, but the result of the reroll must be accepted even if it is worse.

name: Falleen

structuralTraits.name: Hold Breath
structuralTraits.description: Falleen are at home in air or water. A Falleen can hold their breath for a number of rounds equal to 25 times their Constitution score before they need to start making Endurance checks.

structuralTraits.name: Pheromones
structuralTraits.description: As a Standard Action, a Falleen can make a special attack against any adjacent creature. The attack modifier is equal to the Falleen's Character Level plus their Charisma modifier. If the attack equals or exceeds the target's Fortitude Defense, that target moves -1 step along the Condition Track. If the attack fails to beat the target's Fortitude Defense, the target is immune to the Falleen's Pheromones for 24 hours. When the target is reduced to a total of -5 steps, instead of Falling Unconscious, the target's Attitude improves to Friendly. Pheromones function as an Inhaled Poison. Any creature holding its breath is unaffected (see Endurance for more details). A creature can be affected by Falleen Pheromones only once per round. Multiple exposures to Pheromones from different Falleen in the same round produce no additional effects. A Hostile creature cannot be targeted by this effect.

structuralTraits.name: Pheromone Acclimation
structuralTraits.description: Falleen gain a +5 Species bonus to their Fortitude Defense against the Pheromones of other Falleen.

activatedAbilities.name: Pheromones
activatedAbilities.description: As a standard action, a Falleen may impose a -2 penalty to Will Defense on all living creatures within 6 squares until the end of the encounter.

description: Of all the Species to populate the galaxy, none are better known for their intoxicating appearance than the Falleen. An exotic reptilian people, they are favored for their chiseled physiques and entrancing features. These qualities alone would solidify their place among the more handsome Species, but their pheromones make them irresistible to other Species. Their gifts and allure enable the Falleen to move through other cultures with ease, but despite their advantages, Falleen prefer to remain mysterious, withdrawing to their own world in their own system. They do not lack the technology or wherewithal to venture out into the stars, but their cultural predilections make such ventures undesirable.

canonicalStats.abilityText: All Falleen receive a +2 bonus to their Charisma, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Wisdom. Falleen posses uncommon magnetism and great interpersonal skills.

canonicalTraits.name: Hold Breath
canonicalTraits.description: Falleen are at home in air or water. A Falleen can hold their breath for a number of rounds equal to 25 times their Constitution score before they need to start making Endurance checks.

canonicalTraits.name: Pheromones
canonicalTraits.description: As a Standard Action, a Falleen can make a special attack against any adjacent creature. The attack modifier is equal to the Falleen's Character Level plus their Charisma modifier. If the attack equals or exceeds the target's Fortitude Defense, that target moves -1 step along the Condition Track. If the attack fails to beat the target's Fortitude Defense, the target is immune to the Falleen's Pheromones for 24 hours. When the target is reduced to a total of -5 steps, instead of Falling Unconscious, the target's Attitude improves to Friendly. Pheromones function as an Inhaled Poison. Any creature holding its breath is unaffected (see Endurance for more details). A creature can be affected by Falleen Pheromones only once per round. Multiple exposures to Pheromones from different Falleen in the same round produce no additional effects. A Hostile creature cannot be targeted by this effect.

canonicalTraits.name: Pheromone Acclimation
canonicalTraits.description: Falleen gain a +5 Species bonus to their Fortitude Defense against the Pheromones of other Falleen.

name: Feeorin

structuralTraits.name: Intimidating Presence
structuralTraits.description: A Feeorin gains a +5 species bonus on Intimidate checks.

description: In The Old Republic Era, the Feeorin are known to come from the planet Odryn, a planet of severe climatic extremes. Little else is known about the world, given the closed nature of Feeorin society. Few Feeorin venture from their homeworld, and those few outsiders who have survived a visit there tell of a world well suited for the Feeorin, but nobody else. Feeorin are massive humanoids whose aging process allows them to grow stronger and more powerful until they die, dropping dead suddenly of old age. Because they are long-lived, they have ample time to gather and use their strength.

canonicalStats.abilityText: All Feeorin receive a +2 bonus to both their Strength and Constitution, but suffer -2 penalties to their Dexterity, Wisdom, and Charisma. Feeorin are big and brawny, but tend to be blunt and short-tempered.

canonicalTraits.name: Brutal
canonicalTraits.description: Feeorin are vicious combatants who do not hesitate to strike vulnerable areas or fight dirty. Once per encounter, after making a successful melee attack, a Feeorin may treat its opponent's Damage Threshold as though it were 5 points lower when determining the result of the attack.

canonicalTraits.name: Great Fortitude
canonicalTraits.description: Feeorin gain a +2 Species bonus to their Fortitude Defense, accounting for their great physical condition.

canonicalTraits.name: Inborn Resilience
canonicalTraits.description: Feeorin are highly resilient to physical attack. A Feeorin can catch a Second Wind even when unconscious (no Action required, but the Second Wind can only be taken when the Feeorin would normally get a turn). Catching a Second Wind while unconscious also move the Feeorin +3 steps on the Condition Track.

canonicalTraits.name: Low-Light Vision
canonicalTraits.description: Feeorin ignore Concealment from darkness (but not Total Concealment).

canonicalTraits.name: Stronger by Age
canonicalTraits.description: At Middle Age, a Feeorin adds a +1 Species bonus to their Strength and Constitution scores. At Old, this bonus increases to +2. Feeorin do not increase their Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma; nor decrease their Strength, Dexterity, and Constitution as they Age.

name: Felucian

structuralTraits.name: Force Sensitivity
structuralTraits.description: A Felucian may use the Force even if it does not have the Force Sensitivity feat.

description: The Felucians are an unusual, amphibious Species. They are highly adapted to surviving the wilds of their home planet, and fade easily into its confusing mass of plant life. They are equally at home on land or in water, and they traverse the swamps with ease. All Felucians are part of a single, planetwide tribe that is broken down into smaller villages and communities, each one led by Felucian Shamans and chieftains. These Felucian Shamans are very strong in The Force, using it to their own ends with incredible skill.

canonicalStats.abilityText: Felucians receive a +2 bonus to their Constitution, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Intelligence. Felucians must be tough to compete with the dangerous plants and animals of their homeworld, but they live in a relatively primitive state.

canonicalTraits.name: Bonus Feat
canonicalTraits.description: Felucians receive the Force Sensitivity feat at 1st level as a bonus Feat.

canonicalTraits.name: Breathe Underwater
canonicalTraits.description: As amphibious creatures, Felucians can't drown in water.

canonicalTraits.name: Low-Light Vision
canonicalTraits.description: Felucians ignore Concealment (but not Total Concealment) from darkness.

canonicalTraits.name: Natural Camouflage
canonicalTraits.description: A Felucian can choose to reroll any Stealth check to Sneak, keeping the better of the two results.

canonicalTraits.name: Force Blast
canonicalTraits.description: Once per encounter a Felucian can make an Untrained Use the Force check to activate the Force Blast Force Power. A Felucian who takes the Force Training feat automatically adds a single use of the Force Blast Force Power to his or her Force Power Suite (for free) instead of this ability.

canonicalTraits.name: Primitive
canonicalTraits.description: Felucians do not gain Weapon Proficiency (Heavy Weapons), Weapon Proficiency (Pistols), or Weapon Proficiency (Rifles) as starting Feats at first level, even if their Class normally grants them.

canonicalTraits.name: Weapon Familiarity
canonicalTraits.description: Felucians treat the Felucian Skullblade as a Simple Weapon (Melee) instead of an Exotic Weapon (Melee).

name: Fosh

structuralTraits.name: Subtle Influence
structuralTraits.description: A Fosh gains a +5 species bonus on Deception checks.

description: Almost completely unknown by the rest of the galaxy, Fosh are an avianlike Species that hail from an unknown planet. Outwardly quiet, reserved, and thoughtful, in truth Fosh are contemptuous of other Species and consider all non-Fosh to be inferior. Fosh keep this condescension and bigotry to themselves, however. They are remarkably manipulative and are masters of intrigue, deal making, and deception. Only Bothans are more skilled at pulling the strings of others.

canonicalStats.abilityText: All Fosh receive +2 bonuses to their Dexterity and Charisma, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Constitution. Fosh are nimble and persuasive, but they possess frail and delicate bones.

canonicalTraits.name: Deceptive
canonicalTraits.description: A Fosh may reroll any Deception check, but must accept the result of the reroll, even if it is worse.

canonicalTraits.name: Healing Glands
canonicalTraits.description: Fosh can exude a powerful fluid from their tear ducts that can aid in the healing process for most Species. A Fosh is always considered to have a Medpac or Medical Kit for the purposes of making Treat Injury checks, and can perform Trained Only applications of the Treat Injury skill even when Untrained.

name: Gamorrean

structuralTraits.name: Low Cunning
structuralTraits.description: A Gamorrean takes a -2 species penalty on Will Defense.

conditionalTraits.name: Brutal Charge
conditionalTraits.description: A Gamorrean gains a +2 species bonus on melee damage rolls when charging.

description: Gamorreans are green-skinned, porcine creatures native to Gamorr. Their tendency toward violence makes them valued as criminal enforcers; though viewed as mindless brutes, they don't care so long as they are paid to fight. Gamorrean civilization revolves around the never-ending wars between their clans. Preferring large melee weapon in combat, many see ranged weapons as cowardly. Males live to fight; females farm, hunt, and manufacture weapons.

canonicalStats.abilityText: Gamorreans have a +2 bonus to Strength, but suffer a -2 penalty to both their Dexterity and Intelligence. Gamorreans possess greater physical power, but have limited agility and intelligence.

canonicalTraits.name: Bonus Feat
canonicalTraits.description: Gamorreans gain Improved Damage Threshold as a bonus Feat.

canonicalTraits.name: Great Fortitude
canonicalTraits.description: Gamorreans gain a +2 Species bonus to their Fortitude Defense, accounting for their great physical resistance.

canonicalTraits.name: Primitive
canonicalTraits.description: Gamorreans do not gain Weapon Proficiency (Heavy Weapons), Weapon Proficiency (Pistols), or Weapon Proficiency (Rifles) as starting Feats at first level, even if their Class normally grants them.

canonicalTraits.name: Weapon Familiarity (Arg'garok)
canonicalTraits.description: Gamorreans treat the Arg'garok as an Advanced Melee Weapon, instead of an Exotic Weapons (Melee).

name: Gand

structuralTraits.name: Darkvision
structuralTraits.description: Gand ignore Concealment (including Total Concealment) from darkness. However, they cannot perceive colors in total darkness.

structuralTraits.name: Hardy
structuralTraits.description: Gand are tough and resilient, capable of shrugging off injuries that would cripple other Species. Whenever a Gand uses their Second Wind, they regain a number of additional Hit Points equal to 5 + its Character Level.

structuralTraits.name: Limb Regeneration
structuralTraits.description: A Gand can regrow a lost limb in 1d10 days. At the end of this time, all Persistent penalties associated with the loss of the limb are removed.

structuralTraits.name: Special Equipment
structuralTraits.description: The dependence of Gand on the atmosphere of their homeworld restricts their ability to leave without use of specialized equipment. Without a Breathing Apparatus, a Gand would Suffocate. Without a Transliterator, a Gand cannot communicate in any language other than Gand. A Breathing Apparatus costs 2000 credits, and a year's supply of breathing filters cost 200 credits (50 credits on Gand). A Transliterator costs 100 credits. A Gand character begins play with these items at no cost.

structuralTraits.name: Weapon Familiarity
structuralTraits.description: Gand do not treat Gand Weapons as Exotic Weapons, instead treating them as a base item (for instance, a Gand Blaster Rifle would still be treated as a Rifle).

description: A strange Species of insect creatures, the Gand have evolved in response to the toxic atmosphere of their homeworld. In fact, two distinct types of Gand exist. The more common has features typical of other Species- lungs and the usual assortment of internal organs. The other type- rarely found outside Gand- has no lungs, and takes in the nutrients it needs through the materials it consumes. Since the Gand cannot tolerate other atmospheres, and outsiders cannot breathe Gand's atmosphere, the Species of this far-flung world developed without outside interference, resulting in a rich culture with particularities.

canonicalStats.abilityText: All Gand receive a +2 bonus to their Wisdom, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Charisma. Gand society values wisdom and cunning, but their off-putting appearance and strange manner make them difficult to understand.

canonicalTraits.name: Darkvision
canonicalTraits.description: Gand ignore Concealment (including Total Concealment) from darkness. However, they cannot perceive colors in total darkness.

canonicalTraits.name: Hardy
canonicalTraits.description: Gand are tough and resilient, capable of shrugging off injuries that would cripple other Species. Whenever a Gand uses their Second Wind, they regain a number of additional Hit Points equal to 5 + its Character Level.

canonicalTraits.name: Limb Regeneration
canonicalTraits.description: A Gand can regrow a lost limb in 1d10 days. At the end of this time, all Persistent penalties associated with the loss of the limb are removed.

canonicalTraits.name: Special Equipment
canonicalTraits.description: The dependence of Gand on the atmosphere of their homeworld restricts their ability to leave without use of specialized equipment. Without a Breathing Apparatus, a Gand would Suffocate. Without a Transliterator, a Gand cannot communicate in any language other than Gand. A Breathing Apparatus costs 2000 credits, and a year's supply of breathing filters cost 200 credits (50 credits on Gand). A Transliterator costs 100 credits. A Gand character begins play with these items at no cost.

canonicalTraits.name: Weapon Familiarity
canonicalTraits.description: Gand do not treat Gand Weapons as Exotic Weapons, instead treating them as a base item (for instance, a Gand Blaster Rifle would still be treated as a Rifle).

variants.label: Force-Sensitive
variants.description: A strange Species of insect creatures, the Gand have evolved in response to the toxic atmosphere of their homeworld. In fact, two distinct types of Gand exist. The more common has features typical of other Species- lungs and the usual assortment of internal organs. The other type- rarely found outside Gand- has no lungs, and takes in the nutrients it needs through the materials it consumes. Since the Gand cannot tolerate other atmospheres, and outsiders cannot breathe Gand's atmosphere, the Species of this far-flung world developed without outside interference, resulting in a rich culture with particularities.

variants.label: Non-Force-Sensitive
variants.description: A strange Species of insect creatures, the Gand have evolved in response to the toxic atmosphere of their homeworld. In fact, two distinct types of Gand exist. The more common has features typical of other Species- lungs and the usual assortment of internal organs. The other type- rarely found outside Gand- has no lungs, and takes in the nutrients it needs through the materials it consumes. Since the Gand cannot tolerate other atmospheres, and outsiders cannot breathe Gand's atmosphere, the Species of this far-flung world developed without outside interference, resulting in a rich culture with particularities.

name: Gen'Dai

structuralTraits.name: Regeneration
structuralTraits.description: A Gen'Dai automatically regains hit points equal to its level at the start of each turn unless damaged by fire or acid.

description: The Gen'Dai benefit from extremely long life spans. Accounts of Gen'Dai living to be over four thousand standard years old are common. They are a hardy Species, thanks in part to their unique physiology. The Gen'Dai have no centralized vital organs, and their elaborate nervous and vascular systems allow them to act quickly and withstand wounds that would kill nearly any other creature. The Gen'Dai are intergalactic nomads, constantly traversing the galaxy.

canonicalStats.abilityText: All Gen'Dai receive a +4 bonus to their Constitution, but suffer -2 penalties to both their Wisdom and Charisma. Gen'Dai are incredibly resistant to damage, but their long life spans cause them to be mentally unstable.

canonicalTraits.name: Fast Healing
canonicalTraits.description: Whenever a Gen'Dai takes its Second Wind, it regains 5 Hit Points every round at the end of its turn until killed, the end of the encounter, or until reaching one-half Hit Points (whichever comes first).

canonicalTraits.name: Hibernation
canonicalTraits.description: A Gen'Dai can enter a state similar to a Hibernation Trance. A Gen'Dai can use the Force Trance application of the Use the Force skill Untrained (and even if not Force-sensitive) and can remain in the trance indefinitely. A Gen'Dai in such a state ceases to Age, and needs no food, water, or air until it emerges from Hibernation.

canonicalTraits.name: Mental Instability
canonicalTraits.description: As they Age, Gen'Dai begin to lose touch with reality. For each Age Category a Gen'Dai advances past Adult, it takes an additional loss of -1 Wisdom. Gen'Dai characters never gain increased Wisdom due to Age, though they can increase their Wisdom scores as their Character Level increases.

canonicalTraits.name: Regeneration
canonicalTraits.description: A Gen'Dai can regrow lost body parts- including significant portions of its physical structure and individual limbs- in 1d10 minutes. At the end of that time, all Persistent penalties associated with the loss of one or more limbs are removed.

canonicalTraits.name: Resilient Physiology
canonicalTraits.description: Because of their unique physiology, the Gen'Dai can shrug off the effect of wounds that would be fatal to any other Species. Because of this, a Gen'Dai's Damage Threshold is doubled after adding bonuses for Feats and size when determining whether the Gen'Dai is killed by an attack (but not for any other purpose).

name: Geonosian

structuralTraits.name: Wings
structuralTraits.description: A Geonosian may fly with a speed of 6 squares (clumsy).

structuralTraits.name: Hive Mind
structuralTraits.description: A Geonosian gains a +2 species bonus on Initiative checks.

description: The Geonosians are a caste-dominated Species from the world of Geonosis, in the system of the same name. They dwell in a spectacular architectural realm carved from the soaring, rocky spires that dot the planet's landscape. They are somewhat resistant to the Radiation that occasionally showers their world, though Geonosians actually live underground to escape that phenomenon. Geonosians are born into specific castes divided along the lines of their physical attributes, and though most Geonosians are content to remain within their caste until they die, some develop ambition. The life of a low-ranking Geonosian Worker is ordinarily one of ongoing toil, laboring to fulfill the whims of a Geonosian Aristocracy that sometimes makes spectacular demands.

canonicalStats.abilityText: All Geonosians receive a +2 bonus to their Strength, but suffer -2 penalties to both their Intelligence and Charisma. Geonosians are deceptively strong, but their hive-like mentality makes it difficult for them to act on their own.

canonicalTraits.name: Flight
canonicalTraits.description: Geonosians are capable of flight, but cannot do so if they are carrying a Heavy Load.

canonicalTraits.name: Low-Light Vision
canonicalTraits.description: Geonosians ignore Concealment (but not Total Concealment) from darkness.

canonicalTraits.name: Radiation Resistance
canonicalTraits.description: Geonosians receive a +5 Species bonus to their Fortitude Defense against Radiation. In addition, Geonosians never gain a Persistent Condition from the effects of Radiation.

name: Givin

structuralTraits.name: Analytical Mind
structuralTraits.description: A Givin gains a +5 species bonus on Mechanics and Knowledge (physical sciences) checks.

description: The shipyards that circle Yag'Duhl, the Givin homeworld, produce sturdy and elegant starships and compete on a level matched only by the Duros and Verpine, the only Species that receive grudging respect from the Givin. However, Givin designers do not equip their ships with Navicomputers, since most Givin can calculate hyperspace jumps in their heads, and they often overlook life-support systems. Members of other Species that purchase Givin built ships must add these components.

canonicalStats.abilityText: All Givin receive a +2 bonus to their Intelligence, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Strength. Givin lack physical strength, but they are extremely analytical.

canonicalTraits.name: Damage Reduction
canonicalTraits.description: The hard shells of Givin provides a Species bonus of Damage Reduction 2.

canonicalTraits.name: Deductive Reasoning
canonicalTraits.description: Givin constantly evaluate data, discarding inconclusive results. A Givin can reroll any Knowledge check, but the result of the reroll must be accepted, even if it is worse.

canonicalTraits.name: Great Fortitude
canonicalTraits.description: Givin gain a +2 Species bonus to their Fortitude Defense.

canonicalTraits.name: Vacuum Immunity
canonicalTraits.description: Givin have sealed exoskeletons and do not breathe in a conventional sense. They are immune to the effects of Vacuum and to the effects of inhaled chemicals and Poisons.

name: Gotal

structuralTraits.name: Force Awareness
structuralTraits.description: A Gotal gains blindsense out to 6 squares.

description: Emotions are an important part of Gotal culture. Young Gotals live in a constant state of confusion as they are bombarded by the emotions of others. Learning to handle this input is what pushes a Gotal toward adulthood and maturity. Adult Gotals are calm, almost serene beings, but despite their outward appearance, they feel the full breadth of emotions. Because they are so sensitive to emotions, Gotals quickly form meaningful relationships with one another.

canonicalStats.abilityText: Gotals have no Ability Score adjustments, having no particular advantage or disadvantage in mentality or physical prowess.

canonicalTraits.name: Energy Reading
canonicalTraits.description: Gotals can read the emotions of any sentient being within 6 squares. As a Standard Action, a Gotal can make a Perception check against a target's Will Defense. A successful check reveals the target's emotional state (such as happy, sad, nervous, or scared).

canonicalTraits.name: Low-Light Vision
canonicalTraits.description: Gotals ignore Concealment (but not Total Concealment) from darkness.

canonicalTraits.name: Sensor Cones
canonicalTraits.description: A Gotal can use the Sense Force and Sense Surroundings applications of the Use the Force skill Untrained.

name: Gran

structuralTraits.name: Three-Eyed Vision
structuralTraits.description: A Gran gains a +2 species bonus to Reflex Defense.

description: Gran place society over self, and they receive training that complements both their personal talents and society's needs. The desire to maintain balance within society caused the Gran to establish alien-only areas of Kinyen. This was intended to restrict alien influence and avoid trouble between Species, such as what happened on Malastare. During The Dark Times, the Empire has pushed the Gran leadership to grant access to all areas. When the Gran refused, Kinyen was subjected to orbital bombardment.

canonicalStats.abilityText: All Gran receive a +2 bonus to their Charisma, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Intelligence. Gran are highly social creatures, but tend to value artistic value higher than scientific achievement.

canonicalTraits.name: Conditional Bonus Feat
canonicalTraits.description: A Gran with the Point-Blank Shot feat gains the Precise Shot feat as a bonus Feat.

canonicalTraits.name: Darkvision
canonicalTraits.description: Gran ignore Concealment (including Total Concealment) from darkness. However, they cannot perceive colors in total darkness.

canonicalTraits.name: Innate Accuracy
canonicalTraits.description: Once per encounter, a Gran can Aim by taking a single Swift Action instead of two Swift Actions.

canonicalTraits.name: Target Awareness
canonicalTraits.description: A Gran takes no distance penalties on Perception checks made to notice targets for the first 50 squares between the Gran and its targets.

name: Gungan

structuralTraits.name: Amphibious
structuralTraits.description: A Gungan can breathe both air and water.

activatedAbilities.name: Lucky
activatedAbilities.description: Once per encounter, a Gungan may reroll an attack roll, skill check, or ability check but must accept the new result.

description: The Gungans are omnivorous humanoids native to the swamps of Naboo. Technologically advanced, they rely mostly on biotech, growing instead of building their homes and production facilities. While their culture is peaceful, it evolved from a series of long, bloody clan wars, and Gungans still admire strength and cunning. Most communities are devoted to farming or manufacturing goods that are then traded to other Gungan settlements, but they will reluctantly trade with the Humans of Naboo.

canonicalStats.abilityText: Gungans have a natural +2 bonus to their Dexterity, but suffer -2 penalties to their Intelligence and Charisma. Gungans are agile, but aren't particularly witty or astute.

canonicalTraits.name: Expert Swimmer
canonicalTraits.description: A Gungan may choose to reroll any Swim check, but the result of the reroll must be accepted even if it is worse. In addition, a Gungan may choose to Take 10 on Swim checks even when distracted or threatened.

canonicalTraits.name: Hold Breath
canonicalTraits.description: Gungans are at home either in air or in water. A Gungan can hold his or her breath for a number of rounds equal to 25 times their Constitution score before they need to make Endurance checks to hold their breath.

canonicalTraits.name: Lightning Reflexes
canonicalTraits.description: Gungans gain a +2 Species bonus to their Reflex Defense, accounting for their uncanny ability to avoid danger.

canonicalTraits.name: Low-Light Vision
canonicalTraits.description: Gungans ignore Concealment (But not Total Concealment) from darkness.

canonicalTraits.name: Weapon Familiarity
canonicalTraits.description: Gungans treat the Atlatl, Cesta, and Electropole as Simple Weapons (Melee) instead of Exotic Weapons (Melee).

name: Herglic

structuralTraits.name: Massive Build
structuralTraits.description: A Herglic gains a +5 species bonus on Strength checks.

description: Herglics are hulking bipeds that evolved from water-dwelling mammals on the planet Giju. Their ancestors' fins and flukes have been replaced by arms and legs, although Herglics still breathe through blowholes on the tops of their heads. As a whole, Herglics are great believers in fairness and honesty. They are renowned for always being upright traders and for honoring any deal or bargain they make. They assume that others behave in a similar fashion, and few things anger a Herglic more than deceit and cheating.

canonicalStats.abilityText: All Herglics receive a +4 bonus to their Strength, but suffer a -4 penalty to their Dexterity. Herglics are large and strong, but they lack grace and manual dexterity.

canonicalTraits.name: Conditional Bonus Feat
canonicalTraits.description: Herglics are gregarious creatures with a gift for inducing others to open up. A Herglic with Gather Information as a Trained Skill receives Skill Focus (Gather Information) as a bonus Feat.

canonicalTraits.name: Sturdy
canonicalTraits.description: Herglics are solidly built and literally have tough skin. Whenever a Herglic catches a Second Wind, he or she regains a number of additional Hit Points equal to 5 + one-half its level.

name: Houk

structuralTraits.name: Powerful Arms
structuralTraits.description: A Houk gains a +2 species bonus on melee attack rolls.

description: The Houk have well-earned reputations as bullies, cheaters, and backstabbers. They are often compared to Wookiees because of their great strength and violent disposition, although Wookiees are usually more agreeable and trustworthy. The Houk acquired hyperspace technology several thousand years before the Battle of Yavin and spread across the galaxy, seeding many planets with small colonies. During the Rebellion era, Imperial agents captured and experimented on hundreds of Houk colonists, hoping to create strong yet obedient slaves. The Houk did not submit easily, and most of them were never seen again.

canonicalStats.abilityText: All Houk receive a +2 bonus to both their Strength and Constitution, but suffer -2 penalties to both their Wisdom and Charisma. Houk are huge and muscular, but have a reputation for their brutality and aggravation that is not undeserved.

canonicalTraits.name: Natural Armor
canonicalTraits.description: Houk are thick-boned and thick-skinned. They gain a +1 Natural Armor bonus to their Reflex Defense. A Natural Armor bonus stacks with an Armor bonus.

canonicalTraits.name: Physically Intimidating
canonicalTraits.description: Houk use their Strength modifier instead of their Charisma modifier for Persuasion checks made to Intimidate others.

name: Human

structuralTraits.name: Versatile
structuralTraits.description: A Human gains a bonus feat at 1st level.

structuralTraits.name: Skilled
structuralTraits.description: A Human gains one additional trained skill.

description: Humans dominate the Core Worlds and can be found in virtually every corner of the galaxy. They are the ancestors of early spacefaring pioneers, conquerors, traders, travelers, and refugees. Humans have always been people on the move. As a result, they can be found on almost every inhabited planet. As a Species, Humans are physically, culturally, and politically diverse.

canonicalStats.abilityText: None. Humans are wide spread across the galaxy, and each with their own strengths and weaknesses.

canonicalTraits.name: Bonus Feat
canonicalTraits.description: Humans gain one bonus Feat at 1st level.

canonicalTraits.name: Bonus Trained Skill
canonicalTraits.description: Humans are versatile and accomplished at many tasks. A Human character chooses one additional Trained Skill at 1st level. The Skill must be chosen from the character's list of Class Skills.

name: Hutt

structuralTraits.name: Immense Bulk
structuralTraits.description: A Hutt gains a +5 species bonus to Fortitude Defense.

structuralTraits.name: Slow
structuralTraits.description: A Hutt's base speed is reduced by 2 squares.

description: Hutts are long-lived gastropods who occupy a region known as Hutt Space. They are powerful, ruthless beings who continually seek to expand the boundaries of their dominion and the holdings of their individual clans, or Kajidics. Their appetite for power is as insatiable as their appetite for food. Hutts are often found at the center of business and criminal enterprises. Legality (or lack thereof) does not affect whether a Hutt will pursue a venture.

canonicalStats.abilityText: All Hutts receive a +2 bonus to their Strength, Constitution, and Intelligence; but suffer a -6 penalty to their Dexterity. Hutts are strong, hardy, and crafty; but are sluggish and uncoordinated.

canonicalTraits.name: Force Resistance
canonicalTraits.description: A Hutt's sharp mind allows them to resist Force abilities. Hutts receive a +5 bonus to their Will Defense against any use of the Use the Force skill.

canonicalTraits.name: Supreme Stability
canonicalTraits.description: A Hutt's sluglike body structure and lack of any kind of legs, allows a Hutt to become completely immune to being knocked Prone.

canonicalTraits.name: Silver Tongued
canonicalTraits.description: Hutts may be known to be heavy handed in their ventures, but they are equally capable in legal negotiations as they are underground business. A Hutt may choose to reroll any Persuasion check, keeping the better of the two results.

name: Iktotchi

structuralTraits.name: Prescient Vision
structuralTraits.description: An Iktotchi gains a +2 species bonus on Initiative checks.

conditionalTraits.name: Prescient Vision (Reroll)
conditionalTraits.description: An Iktotchi may reroll Initiative checks.

description: Hailing from The Expansion Region world of Iktotchon and its moon, Iktotch, the Iktotchi are a race of precognitive humanoids. All Iktotchi possess this precognitive ability, which manifests as vivid dreams, visions, or waking dreams. Few Iktotchi have control of how or when their precognition manifests or of what it reveals to them. As a Species, they know of the Republic's coming long before their world is visited, and they are prepared to welcome the guests that would usher them into a vast galactic society. Iktotchi are famed for their prowess as pilots, and attribute their success at the helm to nearly imperceptible precognitive inclinations.

canonicalStats.abilityText: Iktotchi receive a +2 bonus to their Constitution, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Charisma. Though they are a hardy Species, the Iktotchi are a quiet and introspective people and have trouble forming friendships.

canonicalTraits.name: Natural Armor
canonicalTraits.description: Iktotchi have tough skin that provides a +1 Natural Armor bonus to their Reflex Defense. A Natural Armor bonus stacks with an Armor bonus.

canonicalTraits.name: Limited Precognition
canonicalTraits.description: Iktotchi are gifted with limited precognition and are subjected to prophetic dreams. Once per encounter, as a Reaction, an Iktotchi can grant themself or any Vehicle the Iktotchi Pilots a +2 Species bonus to Reflex Defense against any single attack.

canonicalTraits.name: Expert Pilot
canonicalTraits.description: An Iktotchi can reroll any Pilot check, but the result of the reroll must be accepted, even if it is worse.

name: Ishi Tib

structuralTraits.name: Breathe Underwater
structuralTraits.description: As amphibious creatures, Ishi Tib can't drown in water.

structuralTraits.name: Ecological Awareness
structuralTraits.description: Due to their conscientiousness and drive to protect the natural environment of their homeworld, an Ishi Tib can choose to reroll any Knowledge (Life Sciences) check, keeping the better of the two results.

structuralTraits.name: Expert Swimmer
structuralTraits.description: An Ishi Tib may choose to reroll any Swim check, but the result of the reroll must be accepted, even if it is worse. In addition, an Ishi Tib may choose to Take 10 on Swim checks, even when distracted or threatened.

structuralTraits.name: Low-Light Vision
structuralTraits.description: Ishi Tib ignore Concealment (but not Total Concealment) from darkness.

structuralTraits.name: Natural Weapons
structuralTraits.description: An Ishi Tib has a Natural Weapon, in the form of a sharp beak. When an Ishi Tib makes an Unarmed attack, it may choose to use its Natural Weapons, dealing 1d6 points of piercing damage with the attack instead of normal Unarmed damage. Ishi Tib are always considered armed with their Natural Weapons.

description: Ishi Tib live in schools- communal groups of thousands of individuals, governed by elected representatives who hold office for one-year terms. An Ishi Tib does not know the identity of his or her birth parents, because each Ishi Tib school assumes responsibility for the welfare of its hatchlings, creating a strong sense of community and promoting the needs of the group over those of an individual. Unlike Species that have wrecked environmental havoc upon their homeworlds, Ishi Tib protect their planet, Tibrin, with extensive laws legislating ecological preservation. During the Clone Wars, the Ishi Tib side with The Confederacy of Independent Systems after Count Dooku liberates their world from the Ishi Tib premier Suribran Tu, although Tibrin is still represented in the Galactic Senate. During the Galactic Civil War, Ishi Tib who join The Rebel Alliance plan raids against the Empire, and several Ishi Tib tactical experts are present at the briefing prior to the Battle of Endor.

canonicalStats.abilityText: All Ishi Tib receive a +2 bonus to their Intelligence. Ishi Tib are problem-solvers, striving to find the most efficient solution, with a minimum drain to their resources.

canonicalTraits.name: Breathe Underwater
canonicalTraits.description: As amphibious creatures, Ishi Tib can't drown in water.

canonicalTraits.name: Ecological Awareness
canonicalTraits.description: Due to their conscientiousness and drive to protect the natural environment of their homeworld, an Ishi Tib can choose to reroll any Knowledge (Life Sciences) check, keeping the better of the two results.

canonicalTraits.name: Expert Swimmer
canonicalTraits.description: An Ishi Tib may choose to reroll any Swim check, but the result of the reroll must be accepted, even if it is worse. In addition, an Ishi Tib may choose to Take 10 on Swim checks, even when distracted or threatened.

canonicalTraits.name: Low-Light Vision
canonicalTraits.description: Ishi Tib ignore Concealment (but not Total Concealment) from darkness.

canonicalTraits.name: Natural Weapons
canonicalTraits.description: An Ishi Tib has a Natural Weapon, in the form of a sharp beak. When an Ishi Tib makes an Unarmed attack, it may choose to use its Natural Weapons, dealing 1d6 points of piercing damage with the attack instead of normal Unarmed damage. Ishi Tib are always considered armed with their Natural Weapons.

name: Ithorian

structuralTraits.name: Nature Affinity
structuralTraits.description: An Ithorian gains a +5 species bonus on Knowledge (life sciences) checks.

activatedAbilities.name: Sonic Bellow
activatedAbilities.description: As a standard action, an Ithorian may emit a sonic bellow that deals 1d6 sonic damage to adjacent enemies.

description: Ithorians are tall humanoids whose appearance leads many to refer to them colloquially as "Hammerheads." Peaceful and gentle, Ithorians are widely recognized as talented artists, brilliant agricultural engineers, and skilled diplomats. Ithorians are perhaps the greatest ecologists in the galaxy, devoting their technology to preserving the natural beauty of their homeworld's jungles. They live in "Herds," dwelling in cities that hover above the surface of their planet and striving to maintain the ecological balance in the "Mother Jungle." Ithorians also travel the galaxy in massive "Herd Ships," masterpieces of environmental engineering that carry a perfect replica of their native jungle. Many look forward to trading for the exotic wares the Ithorians bring from distant planets.

canonicalStats.abilityText: All Ithorians receive a +2 bonus to both their Wisdom and Charisma, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Dexterity. Ithorians are wise and deliberate thinkers, whose personable nature allows them to get along well with others, but they are not agile.

canonicalTraits.name: Bellow
canonicalTraits.description: As a Standard Action, an Ithorian can open all four of its throats and emit a terrible subsonic Bellow. The Ithorian makes a special attack roll (1d20 + its Character Level) and compares the result to the Fortitude Defense of all creatures and unattended objects in a 6-Square Cone. A successful hit deals 3d6 points of Sonic damage; if the attack misses, the target takes half damage instead. Each use of this ability moves the Ithorian -1 step along the Condition Track. An Ithorian can choose to add more dice (d6s) to the damage it deals with its Bellow, but each additional 1d6 of damage moves the Ithorian another -1 step along the Condition Track.

canonicalTraits.name: Conditional Bonus Feat
canonicalTraits.description: Ithorians like to maintain close ties with nature. An Ithorian with Knowledge (Life Sciences) as a Trained Skill gains Skill Focus (Knowledge (Life Sciences)) as a bonus Feat.

canonicalTraits.name: Iron Will
canonicalTraits.description: Ithorians have above-average willpower and gain a +2 Species bonus to their Will Defense.

canonicalTraits.name: Survival Instinct
canonicalTraits.description: An Ithorian may choose to reroll any Survival check, but the result of the reroll must be accepted, even if it is worse.

name: Jawa

structuralTraits.name: Scavenger
structuralTraits.description: A Jawa gains a +5 species bonus on Mechanics checks to repair or jury-rig devices.

description: Jawas are intelligent and have a complex society. Gathering in extended tribes, Jawa life revolves around trade- not for wealth, but for survival. Jawas look for guidance from their shamans, usually a female Jawa with the ability to portent the future and cast vicious hexes. Once a year, the Jawa tribes gather in the Dune Sea to exchange sons and daughters, to swap stories and news, and to mingle among their own kind.

variants.label: Alternate
variants.description: Alternate Jawa trait profile from the canonical species text.

traits.name: Desert Dweller
traits.description: Jawas may Take 10 on Survival checks in desert terrain.

traits.name: Tinkerer
traits.description: A Jawa can choose to reroll any Mechanics check made to Repair an object, vehicle, or droid, but must keep the second result, even if it is worse.

traits.name: Darkvision
traits.description: Jawas ignore Concealment (including Total Concealment) from darkness. However, they cannot perceive colors in total darkness.

canonicalStats.abilityText: All Jawas receive a +2 bonus to their Dexterity, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Strength. Jawas are quick and agile, but they lack the strength of larger creatures.

canonicalTraits.name: Darkvision
canonicalTraits.description: Jawas ignore Concealment (including Total Concealment) from darkness. However, they cannot perceive colors in total darkness.

canonicalTraits.name: Desert Dweller
canonicalTraits.description: When making Survival checks to resist the effects of Extreme Heat, Jawas can reroll the check, keeping the better of the two results.

canonicalTraits.name: Scavenger
canonicalTraits.description: Whenever a Jawa armed with an Ion weapon successfully damages a Droid or Vehicle, the Jawa adds half their Character Level in Ion damage.

canonicalTraits.name: Tinkerer
canonicalTraits.description: A Jawa can choose to reroll any Mechanics check, but must keep the second result, even if it is worse. In addition, Mechanics is always a Class Skill for Jawas.

name: Kaleesh

structuralTraits.name: Fearsome Reputation
structuralTraits.description: A Kaleesh gains a +5 species bonus on Intimidate checks.

description: The Kaleesh are a nomadic Species with a tribal culture. They are spiritual beings who worship their ancestors as deities, and they consider their ancestral burial grounds to be sacred lands. The Kaleesh believe that anyone who dies in honorable service to their people becomes a god upon death. The Kaleesh come to a momentary prominence in the galaxy when they fight off an invasion by an alien Species known as the Huk. Having already subjugated and exploited the natural resources of several worlds, the Huk capture millions of Kaleesh and sell them into slavery.

canonicalStats.abilityText: All Kaleesh receive a +2 bonus to both their Constitution and Charisma scores, but suffer a -2 penalty to both their Dexterity and Intelligence scores. Kaleesh are sturdy and work well with others, but lack sophistication and precision.

canonicalTraits.name: Conditional Bonus Feat
canonicalTraits.description: Kaleesh are a nomadic people who have learned to live off the land. A Kaleesh with Survival as a Trained Skill gains Skill Focus (Survival) as a bonus Feat.

canonicalTraits.name: Darkvision
canonicalTraits.description: Because of the thermoreceptor glands next to their eyes, Kaleesh ignore Concealment (including Total Concealment) from darkness. However, they cannot perceive colors in total darkness.

canonicalTraits.name: Driven
canonicalTraits.description: Kaleesh are focused individuals who maintain sight of their goals even under adverse conditions. Because of this single-minded drive, they gain a +5 Species bonus to their Will Defense against Mind-Affecting effects.

canonicalTraits.name: Persistent
canonicalTraits.description: A Kaleesh can reroll any Endurance check, but the result of the reroll must be accepted, even if it is worse.

name: Kaminoan

structuralTraits.name: Genetic Expertise
structuralTraits.description: A Kaminoan gains a +5 species bonus on Knowledge (life sciences) checks.

structuralTraits.name: Fragile
structuralTraits.description: A Kaminoan takes a -2 species penalty to Fortitude Defense.

description: The watery planet of Kamino is home to a Species of cloners known as Kaminoans. These tall aliens are the result of generations of selective breeding and genetic modification that began when their world suffered a drastic change in climate. These restrictive breeding programs have made Kaminoans an extremely adaptable Species. Apart from gender, Kaminoans have little variation, and to the untrained eye they all look the same. Subtle variations have been introduced into their genetic codes, however, allowing for diversity within a strict set of parameters.

variants.label: Alternate
variants.description: Alternate Kaminoan trait profile from the canonical species text.

traits.name: Ocean Dweller
traits.description: Kaminoans may Take 10 on Survival checks in aquatic terrain.

traits.name: Reroll
traits.description: Kaminoans can choose to reroll any Treat Injury or Knowledge (Life Sciences) check, but must keep the second result, even if it is worse.

canonicalStats.abilityText: All Kaminoans receive a +2 bonus to their Intelligence, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Wisdom. Kaminoans are smart, largely emphasizing on education, but their arrogance tends to blind them to their flaws.

canonicalTraits.name: Conditional Bonus Feat
canonicalTraits.description: Kaminoans are expert cloners and posses remarkable knowledge about the physiologies of Species across the galaxy. A Kaminoan with Treat Injury as a Trained Skill gains Skill Focus (Treat Injury) as a bonus Feat.

canonicalTraits.name: Resilient
canonicalTraits.description: Scientific refinements to their genetic code makes Kaminoans resistant to hazards that target their physiology. Kaminoans gain a +1 Species bonus to their Fortitude Defense.

name: Kel Dor

structuralTraits.name: Force Awareness
structuralTraits.description: A Kel Dor gains a +2 species bonus on Use the Force checks.

notes: A Kel Dor must wear a breath mask to survive in oxygen-rich atmospheres.
description: The Kel Dor evolved on Dorin, a world with an atmosphere consisting mostly of helium and a gas that is unique to that world. As such, Kel Dor cannot breathe on planets with common nitrogen/oxygen atmospheres. Conversely, Dorin's atmosphere is toxic to most nonnative life. On other planets, Kel Dor dwellings provide their native atmosphere (stored in large tanks). When outside, Kel Dor must wear breath masks and goggles.

canonicalStats.abilityText: All Kel Dor receive a +2 bonus to both their Dexterity and Wisdom, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Constitution. Kel Dor are quick and wise, but they suffer from weaker constitutions than others.

canonicalTraits.name: Keen Force Sense
canonicalTraits.description: Kel Dor may choose to reroll any Use the Force check made to Search Your Feelings or Sense Force, keeping the better of the two results.

canonicalTraits.name: Low-Light Vision
canonicalTraits.description: Kel Dor ignore Concealment (but not Total Concealment) from darkness.

canonicalTraits.name: Special Equipment
canonicalTraits.description: Kel Dor suffer limitations outside their native atmosphere. Without protective goggles, a Kel Dor is considered Blind. Without an Antiox Breath Mask, a Kel Dor begins to Suffocate. A replacement mask costs 2,000 credits (500 on Dorin), and a year's supply of filters costs 200 credits (50 on Dorin). Kel Dor characters begin play with these items at no cost. An Antiox Breath Mask is poisonous to other Species.

name: Kerkoiden

structuralTraits.name: Military Training
structuralTraits.description: A Kerkoiden gains a +2 species bonus on Initiative checks.

description: A smaller Species from The Expansion Region, the Kerkoiden are aggressive and cunning beings who often have something to prove. Excellent traders, skilled politicians, and creative military commanders, Kerkoiden are known as formidable opponents, a reputation they prove is well-earned during the Clone Wars. Xenobiologists from the University of Sanbra believe that the Kerkoiden Species descended from quadruped predators that hunt on the savannahs and in the jungles of Kerkoidia. Much of Kerkoiden physiology seems to support this claim, including their hunched posture, long snout, and jutting teeth. Additionally, Kerkoiden aggression seems to be consistent with the predatory instinct of their ancestors, lending credence to this theory of Kerkoiden evolution.

canonicalStats.abilityText: Kerkoiden receive a +2 bonus to their Wisdom, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Constitution. Though less hardy than other Species, Kerkoiden are devious and cunning.

canonicalTraits.name: Predator's Heritage
canonicalTraits.description: Despite their stature, Kerkoiden are descended from predatory beings. Kerkoiden gain a +2 Species bonus on damage rolls against a target that has been damaged since the beginning of the Kerkoiden's last turn.

canonicalTraits.name: Social Cunning
canonicalTraits.description: Kerkoiden may use their Wisdom modifier in place of their Charisma modifier for determining their bonus on Deception and Persuasion checks.

name: Khil

structuralTraits.name: Force Sensitivity
structuralTraits.description: A Khil may use the Force even if it does not have the Force Sensitivity feat.

description: Even in The Old Republic Era, the Khil are considered to be one of the oldest Species of the Republic. They are avid travelers and settlers, spreading colonies and settlements throughout the galaxy. During this era, the Khil are particularly active colonizers. They respect planets already inhabited by native sapient Species or other colonists, preferring to use worlds without significant major threats, be they intelligent or highly predatory. However, once a colony is established, the Khil become fully dedicated to its success.

canonicalStats.abilityText: Khil receive +2 bonuses to their Intelligence and Charisma, but take a -2 penalty to their Constitution. Khil are charming and well-learned, but their endurance leaves much to be desired.

canonicalTraits.name: Cooperative Spirit
canonicalTraits.description: Khil work with others easily. They can Aid Another on Skill Checks made by adjacent allies as a Swift Action, and others can likewise aid Khil on Skill Checks as Swift Actions when adjacent to the Khil. This ability only applies to Climb, Endurance, Gather Information, Jump, Knowledge, Mechanics, Pilot, Ride, Survival, Swim, Treat Injury, or Use Computer skill checks.

canonicalTraits.name: Dependable Worker
canonicalTraits.description: Khil reliably achieve good results. Once per encounter, a Khil can treat one Skill Check with certain Skills (Climb, Endurance, Gather Information, Jump, Knowledge, Mechanics, Pilot, Ride, Survival, Swim, Treat Injury, or Use Computer) as though he or she had Taken 10, even after the roll has been made (but before the result of the check is known).

canonicalTraits.name: Self-Sustaining
canonicalTraits.description: Khil are able to absorb nutrition through the tentacles on their faces, called hullepi, drawing nourishment from almost any environment. Khil do not suffer the effect of Starvation, except in sterile environments.

structuralTraits.name: Psychometry
structuralTraits.description: A Kiffar gains a +2 species bonus on Use the Force checks to sense Force impressions.

name: Killik

structuralTraits.name: Hive Mind
structuralTraits.description: Killiks share a Hive Mind and use their antennae to communicate by telepathy with other members of their Species. A Killik within 1 kilometer of another Killik can communicate with it via Telepathy.

structuralTraits.name: Joiners
structuralTraits.description: Whenever a Killik reduces a living creature with an Intelligence score of 3 or higher to 0 Hit Points, or the Killik knocks the creature unconscious by moving it down the Condition Track, the Killik can transform the target into a Joiner. The target must be Helpless for this to occur. The process takes 1 minute and exposes the target to mind-altering pheromones. Once the target becomes a Joiner, its Attitude shifts to Friendly to the Killik and its allies, and the target gains a +10 bonus to its Will Defense against attempts to use Persuasion to alter its perceptions of its new Killik friends. A target transformed into a Joiner in this manner can make a DC 20 Intelligence check once per day to remove the effect and return to its original Attitude. This is a Mind-Affecting effect.

structuralTraits.name: Low-Light Vision
structuralTraits.description: Killiks ignore Concealment (but not Total Concealment) from darkness.

structuralTraits.name: Multiple Limbs
structuralTraits.description: Killiks have four limbs that are generally used as arms and hands. When making multiple attacks, a Killik reduces the penalty to its attack rolls by 2.

structuralTraits.name: Natural Armor
structuralTraits.description: A Killik's chitinous exoskeleton provides a +1 Natural Armor bonus to its Reflex Defense. A Natural Armor bonus stacks with an Armor bonus.

structuralTraits.name: Natural Weapons
structuralTraits.description: A Killik has Natural Weapons, in the form of three-fingered claws. When a Killik makes an Unarmed attack, it may choose to use its Natural Weapons, dealing 1d6 points of slashing damage with the attack instead of normal Unarmed damage. Killiks are always considered armed with their Natural Weapons.

structuralTraits.name: Size
structuralTraits.description: Killik sizes typically range from Small to Huge, with a few reaching the extremes of Fine and Colossal. When creating a Killik, apply the appropriate size modifiers per the table below.

structuralTraits.name: Additional Traits
structuralTraits.description: To include additional traits, see the Creature Generator. With minimal interest in acquiring wealth, power, or fame, they seek wisdom through exploration and meditation. Although they are an ancient spacefaring Species, Krevaaki are rarely encountered in the galaxy at large. Even during The Old Republic Era- their most active period- groups of more than a few Krevaaki are seldom seen at any one location. Krevaaki In response to other Species' xenophobia, the Krevaaki cloak their tentacled bodies with heavy robes or similar garments, leaving only their two dominant tentacles exposed like humanoid arms. More than a few beings have been caught unaware by the sudden appearance of additional tentacles from beneath the folds of a Krevaaki's garments.

description: Killiks are a genetically diverse population of intelligent insects. Their society is composed of hives, also called nests. Members of each hive share the same physical characteristics and possess the ability to communicate with others through pheromones, electromagnetic transmissions, and long-distance telepathy. Multiple nests united under a hive-mind link can be considered a single organism. Its members always refer to themselves in the third person, and each is aware of anything that has been experienced by any other member.

canonicalStats.abilityText: All Killiks receive a +2 bonus to their Constitution, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Intelligence. Killiks are a hardy but intensely focused Species.

canonicalTraits.name: Hive Mind
canonicalTraits.description: Killiks share a Hive Mind and use their antennae to communicate by telepathy with other members of their Species. A Killik within 1 kilometer of another Killik can communicate with it via Telepathy.

A target transformed into a Joiner in this manner can make a DC 20 Intelligence check once per day to remove the effect and return to its original Attitude. This is a Mind-Affecting effect.

Killiks are always considered armed with their Natural Weapons.

canonicalTraits.description: Killik sizes typically range from Small to Huge, with a few reaching the extremes of Fine and Colossal. When creating a Killik, apply the appropriate size modifiers per the table below.

canonicalTraits.description: To include additional traits, see the Creature Generator. With minimal interest in acquiring wealth, power, or fame, they seek wisdom through exploration and meditation. Although they are an ancient spacefaring Species, Krevaaki are rarely encountered in the galaxy at large. Even during The Old Republic Era- their most active period- groups of more than a few Krevaaki are seldom seen at any one location. Krevaaki In response to other Species' xenophobia, the Krevaaki cloak their tentacled bodies with heavy robes or similar garments, leaving only their two dominant tentacles exposed like humanoid arms. More than a few beings have been caught unaware by the sudden appearance of additional tentacles from beneath the folds of a Krevaaki's garments.

name: Kissai

structuralTraits.name: Inspired
structuralTraits.description: Kissai make the best use of any inspiration or information provided to them. Whenever a Kissai receives a morale or insight bonus from any source, the bonus increases by +1.

structuralTraits.name: Warrior's Awareness
structuralTraits.description: Kissai grow up in a violent and warlike society, so they constantly watch for threats. Once per encounter as a Reaction, a Kissai can gain the benefit of the Uncanny Dodge I and Uncanny Dodge II Talents until the beginning of the Kissai's next turn.

structuralTraits.name: Weapon Familiarity
structuralTraits.description: Kissai treat the Massassi Lanvarok as a Simple Weapon (Ranged) instead of an Exotic Weapon (Ranged).

conditionalTraits.name: Dark Ritualist
conditionalTraits.description: A Kissai gains a +2 species bonus on Use the Force checks when using dark side powers.

description: The Kissai are just as ruthless as the Massassi, but they are more intelligent and observant. Their strict cultural standards demand that they serve their masters well, a trait instilled in them by the Dark Jedi who first enslaved their Species. When the Massassi and Kissai flee Korriban (well before the arrival of the eventual Sith Lords) to resettle on Ziost, the Kissai oversee the rebuilding efforts. The Kissai serve The Sith directly, but the Sith Lord Naga Sadow has other ideas for the Massassi caste. The Kissai are largely left on Ziost as the servants of other Sith Lords, but Sadow uses Sith Alchemy to transform the Massassi into his hulking, brutish soldiers.

canonicalStats.abilityText: All Kissai receive a +2 bonus to their Charisma, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Wisdom. Kissai are charming and pleasant, but their devotion to The Sith blinds them to the suffering that follows.

name: Klatooinian

structuralTraits.name: Hard Laborer
structuralTraits.description: A Klatooinian gains a +2 species bonus on Endurance checks.

description: The ancient Klatooinians perceived the Hutts as demigods. The Hutts, never a kind to refuse the upper hand in any situation, used this false perception to their own advantage, convincing the Klatooinians to cast aside their ancient traditions, abandon their native language in favor of Huttese, and accept their place as indentured servants. Over the millennia, the Hutts have sheltered the Klatooinians and cultivated a society that is largely docile, obedient, illiterate, and isolated from the greater galactic community. Exposure to technology has not diminished the Klatooinian predilection for simplicity. However, youthful Klatooinians often chafe at the conventional "Wisdom" of their elders, and despite the Hutts' efforts to shield their Klatooinian subjects from progressive ideas, many Klatooinians strive to break away from their Hutt overlords.

canonicalStats.abilityText: All Klatooinians receive a +2 bonus to their Constitution, but suffer -2 penalties to both their Wisdom and Intelligence. Life on Klatooine has made the Klatooinians hardy, but they are slow to figure things out and often rely on others to guide them.

canonicalTraits.name: Iron Will
canonicalTraits.description: Klatooinians are resolute individuals and gain a +2 Species bonus to their Will Defense.

name: Krevaaki

structuralTraits.name: Conditional Bonus Feat
structuralTraits.description: A Krevaaki's tentacles give it an advantage in climbing. A Krevaaki who has Climb as a Trained Skill gains Skill Focus (Climb) as a bonus Feat.

structuralTraits.name: Carapace
structuralTraits.description: Krevaaki gain a +1 Natural Armor bonus to their Reflex Defense and a +1 Natural Armor bonus to their Fortitude Defense. A Natural Armor bonus stacks with an Armor bonus.

structuralTraits.name: Stoic
structuralTraits.description: Krevaaki physiology and their limited ability to use facial expressions make them difficult for other Species to read accurately. Any creature attempting to use the Sense Deception or Sense Influence applications of the Perception skill on a Krevaaki takes a -5 penalty to the skill check.

structuralTraits.name: Grasping Tentacles
structuralTraits.description: Krevaaki use their two dominant tentacles like the arms and hands of a humanoid. Krevaaki gain a +2 Species bonus to Climb checks and a +2 Species bonus to Grab and Grapple checks.

description: In response to other Species' xenophobia, the Krevaaki cloak their tentacled bodies with heavy robes or similar garments, leaving only their two dominant tentacles exposed like humanoid arms. More than a few beings have been caught unaware by the sudden appearance of additional tentacles from beneath the folds of a Krevaaki's garments.

canonicalStats.abilityText: All Krevaaki receive a +2 bonus to their Wisdom, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Charisma. Krevaaki are kind-hearted and spiritual, but their unusual physiques tends to unnerve other Species.

name: Lugubraa

structuralTraits.name: Accelerated Learning
structuralTraits.description: Before 50 years of age, a Lugubraa can have a maximum Intelligence score of 8. Around 50 years of age, a Lugubraa's Intelligence score naturally increases by 1d4+2 points.

structuralTraits.name: Bonus Feat
structuralTraits.description: All Lugubraa gain the Improved Damage Threshold feat at 1st level as a bonus Feat.

structuralTraits.name: Darkvision
structuralTraits.description: Although Lugubraa cannot see, their echolocation and heat detection abilities grant them the effects of Darkvision. Lugubraa ignore Concealment (including Total Concealment) from darkness. However, they cannot perceive colors in total darkness.

structuralTraits.name: Lockbite
structuralTraits.description: A Lugubraa that makes a successful Grapple attack can automatically latch onto a target with its teeth, giving the Lugubraa a +5 bonus to subsequent Grapple checks against the target.

structuralTraits.name: Natural Weapons
structuralTraits.description: A Lugubraa has Natural Weapons, in the form of barbed tentacles. When a Lugubraa makes an Unarmed attack, it may choose to use its Natural Weapons, dealing 1d6 points of damage with the attack instead of normal Unarmed damage. Lugubraa are always considered armed with their Natural Weapons.

description: From birth to six months, Lugubraa have the self-awareness of domesticated beasts, able to follow simple commands and steadfastly devoted to their horde or the owner that has purchased their services. After six months, their intelligence reaches that of simple-minded sentients, allowing them to communicate in short sentences but still rendering them hopeless when it comes to advanced calculations. It is rare for Lugubraa to survive more than a few decades, but those that do undergo a second cognitive kick-start at approximately age 50. These unusual geniuses become leaders and strategists for the Lugubraa hordes, plotting ways to acquire more resources and brokering the services of their species as mercenaries to interested galactic parties.

canonicalStats.abilityText: All Lugubraa receive a +2 bonus to both their Strength and Constitution, but suffer -2 penalties to both their Dexterity and Wisdom. Lugubraa are tough but lack the intelligence of most sentient Species.

canonicalTraits.name: Accelerated Learning
canonicalTraits.description: Before 50 years of age, a Lugubraa can have a maximum Intelligence score of 8. Around 50 years of age, a Lugubraa's Intelligence score naturally increases by 1d4+2 points.
