Lugubraa are always considered armed with their Natural Weapons.

name: Lurmen

structuralTraits.name: Pacifist Resolve
structuralTraits.description: A Lurmen gains a +2 species bonus to Will Defense.

description: Lurmen are small, furry humanoids with the ability to roll up in a ball faster overland travel. Their love of peace leads the Lurmen to avoid entanglements in galactic events. As a society, Lurmen eschew conflict of any kind, refusing to fight even in their own defense. During the Clone Wars, a colony of Lurmen on Maridun is nearly wiped out by a Separatist weapon, and only when several Lurmen break away from the wishes of their elders and join the fight are their people saved. Although the Lurmen seem stubborn in holding onto their ways, in truth they lead a lifestyle largely free of conflict, and have for eons.

canonicalStats.abilityText: All Lurmen receive a +2 bonus to their Wisdom, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Strength. Lurmen are pacifists and healers, lacking the physical strength of other Species.

canonicalTraits.name: Expert Climber
canonicalTraits.description: Lurmen are great climbers and may choose to Take 10 on Climb checks even when distracted or threatened.

canonicalTraits.name: Mender
canonicalTraits.description: Lurmen have learned to heal wounds rather than create them. A Lurmen Trained in Treat Injury reduces the Treat Injury DC for all applications of the skill by his or her Wisdom modifier.

canonicalTraits.name: Primitive
canonicalTraits.description: Lurmen do not gain Weapon Proficiency (Heavy Weapons), Weapon Proficiency (Pistols), or Weapon Proficiency (Rifles) as starting Feats at first level, even if their Class normally grants them.

canonicalTraits.name: Roller
canonicalTraits.description: A Lurmen can curl into a ball and quickly roll along the ground. As a Swift Action, a Lurmen can increase his or her base Speed by 4 squares. In this form, the Lurmen can only take the Move, Withdraw, Catch a Second Wind, Drop an Item, Recover, and Run Actions. The Lurmen can also spend a Swift Action to emerge from their rolling form, losing the +4 Speed bonus and regaining access to all Actions he or she can normally take.

name: Mandalorian (Human Variant)

structuralTraits.name: Bonus Feat
structuralTraits.description: Humans gain one bonus Feat at 1st level.

structuralTraits.name: Bonus Trained Skill
structuralTraits.description: Humans are versatile and accomplished at many tasks. A Human character chooses one additional Trained Skill at 1st level. The Skill must be chosen from the character's list of Class Skills.

description: Humans dominate the Core Worlds and can be found in virtually every corner of the galaxy. They are the ancestors of early spacefaring pioneers, conquerors, traders, travelers, and refugees. Humans have always been people on the move. As a result, they can be found on almost every inhabited planet. As a Species, Humans are physically, culturally, and politically diverse.

canonicalStats.abilityText: None. Humans are wide spread across the galaxy, and each with their own strengths and weaknesses.

canonicalTraits.name: Bonus Feat
canonicalTraits.description: Humans gain one bonus Feat at 1st level.

canonicalTraits.name: Bonus Trained Skill
canonicalTraits.description: Humans are versatile and accomplished at many tasks. A Human character chooses one additional Trained Skill at 1st level. The Skill must be chosen from the character's list of Class Skills.

name: Mantellian Savrip

structuralTraits.name: Ability Modifiers
structuralTraits.description: All Mantellian Savrips receive a +4 bonus to both their Strength and Constitution, but suffer -4 penalties to their Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma, as well as a -2 penalty to their Dexterity.

structuralTraits.name: Large Size
structuralTraits.description: As Large creatures, Mantellian Savrips take a -1 size penalty to their Reflex Defense and a -5 size penalty on Stealth checks, and they gain a +5 size bonus to their Damage Threshold. Their lifting and carrying limits are double those of Medium characters.

structuralTraits.name: Speed
structuralTraits.description: Mantellian Savrips have a base speed of 8 squares.

structuralTraits.name: Natural Armor
structuralTraits.description: Mantellian Savrips gain a +2 Natural Armor bonus to their Reflex Defense. A Natural Armor bonus stacks with an Armor bonus.

structuralTraits.name: Natural Weapons
structuralTraits.description: A Mantellian Savrip has Natural Weapons, in the form of tough claws. When making an Unarmed attack, it may choose to use its Natural Weapons, dealing 1d6 points of Slashing damage instead of normal Unarmed damage. Mantellian Savrips are always considered armed with their Natural Weapons.

structuralTraits.name: Poison
structuralTraits.description: If the Natural Weapons of a Mantellian Savrip deal damage to a living target, the target is also Poisoned. If the Poison succeeds on an attack roll (1d20 + Character Level) against the target's Fortitude Defense, the target moves -1 step along the Condition Track. A target moved to the end of the Condition Track by the Poison is Immobilized, but not Unconscious.

structuralTraits.name: Primitive
structuralTraits.description: Mantellian Savrips do not gain Weapon Proficiency (Heavy Weapons), Weapon Proficiency (Pistols), or Weapon Proficiency (Rifles) as starting Feats at first level, even if their Class normally grants them.

structuralTraits.name: Automatic Languages
structuralTraits.description: All Mantellian Savrips can speak Savrip.

activatedAbilities.name: Rage
activatedAbilities.description: Once per day, a Mantellian Savrip can fly into a Rage as a Swift Action. While Raging, the Mantellian Savrip temporarily gains a +2 Rage bonus on melee attack rolls and melee damage rolls, but cannot use Skills that require patience and concentration (Mechanics, Stealth, Use the Force). A fit of Rage lasts for a number of rounds equal to 5 + the Mantellian Savrip's Constitution modifier. At the end of its Rage, the Mantellian Savrip moves -1 Persistent step along the Condition Track. The penalties persist until the Mantellian Savrip takes at least 10 minutes to recuperate.

description: A reptilian Species of semi-sentient brutes native to the planet Ord Mantell, Mantellian Savrips' sentience was a contentious subject to xenobiologist from the Core Worlds. Often believed to be simply a very cunning non-sentient beast by many Mantellians, the Mantellian Savrips are technologically primitive, and have their own informal language. Preferring to wear clothing and wielding simple tools and Weapons, some Mantellian Savrips had learned to speak accented Basic. Mantellian Savrips are naturally inclined to combat, and this, coupled with their unusual language, only fostered the notion that they were unintelligent. Mantellian During the colonization of Ord Mantell, sanctioned killings of the Mantellian Savrip Species nearly drove the race to extinction.

canonicalStats.abilities: +4 Str, +4 Con, -4 Int, -4 Wis, -4 Cha, -2 Dex
canonicalStats.abilityText: All Mantellian Savrips receive a +4 bonus to both their Strength and Constitution, but suffer -4 penalties to their Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma, as well as a -2 penalty to their Dexterity. Mantellian Savrips are solid walls of muscle and power, but are unsophisticated and quick to anger.

canonicalTraits.name: Natural Armor
canonicalTraits.description: Mantellian Savrips have a tough hide that protects them from harm. They gain a +2 Natural Armor bonus to their Reflex Defense. A Natural Armor bonus stacks with an Armor bonus.

canonicalTraits.name: Natural Weapons
canonicalTraits.description: A Mantellian Savrip has Natural Weapons, in the form of tough claws. When a Mantellian Savrip makes an Unarmed attack, it may choose to use its Natural Weapons, dealing 1d6 points of Slashing damage with the attack instead of normal Unarmed damage. Mantellian Savrips are always considered armed with their Natural Weapons.

canonicalTraits.name: Poison
canonicalTraits.description: If the Natural Weapons of a Mantellian Savrip can deal damage to a living target, the target is also Poisoned. If the Poison succeeds on an attack roll (1d20 + the Mantellian Savrip's Character Level) against the target's Fortitude Defense, the target moves -1 step along the Condition Track. A target moved to the end of the Condition Track by the Poison is Immobilized, but not Unconscious.

canonicalTraits.name: Primitive
canonicalTraits.description: Mantellian Savrips do not gain Weapon Proficiency (Heavy Weapons), Weapon Proficiency (Pistols), or Weapon Proficiency (Rifles) as starting Feats at first level, even if their Class normally grants them.

canonicalTraits.name: Rage
canonicalTraits.description: Once per day, a Mantellian Savrip can fly into a Rage as a Swift Action. While Raging, the Mantellian Savrip temporarily gains a +2 Rage bonus on melee attack rolls and melee damage rolls, but cannot use Skills that require patience and concentration, such as Mechanics, Stealth, or Use the Force. A fit of Rage lasts for a number of rounds equal to 5 + the Mantellian Savrip's Constitution modifier. At the end of its Rage, the Mantellian Savrip moves -1 Persistent step along the Condition Track. The penalties imposed by this Persistent Condition persist until the Mantellian Savrip takes at least 10 minutes to recuperate, during which time the Mantellian Savrip can't engage in any strenuous activity.

name: Massassi

structuralTraits.name: Brutal Strength
structuralTraits.description: A Massassi gains a +2 species bonus on melee damage rolls.

conditionalTraits.name: Dark Side Affinity
conditionalTraits.description: A Massassi gains a +2 species bonus on Use the Force checks when using dark side powers.

description: A thousand years after Sadow and the Massassi arrive on Yavin 4, Exar Kun discovers the ancient Sith secrets in the temples. He uses the Massassi to fight the Jedi across the galaxy. Eventually, Kun consumes the life force of every Massassi on Yavin 4 in his transformation into a Dark Side Spirit. A few Massassi are taken offworld prior to Kun's final decimation of their society. Some remain loyal and find their way back to the new Sith Empire, but a few resentful individuals drift into the darker fringes of society, looking to live their own lives or seeking revenge against the Sith.

canonicalStats.abilityText: All Massassi receive a +4 bonus to their Strength, but suffer -2 penalties to their Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma. Massassi are brawny and powerful, but lack the patience and mental strength of their Kissai cousins.

canonicalTraits.name: Conditional Bonus Feat
canonicalTraits.description: Massassi are skilled trackers. A Massassi with Survival as a Trained Skill gains Skill Focus (Survival) as a bonus Feat.

canonicalTraits.name: Duty Bound
canonicalTraits.description: Once per encounter when a Massassi receives a morale bonus of any kind from any source, the Massassi gains Bonus Hit Points equal to 4 x the Massassi's Character Level until the end of the encounter.

canonicalTraits.name: Primitive
canonicalTraits.description: Massassi do not gain Weapon Proficiency (Heavy Weapons), Weapon Proficiency (Pistols), or Weapon Proficiency (Rifles) as starting Feats at first level, even if their Class normally grants them.

canonicalTraits.name: Warrior's Awareness
canonicalTraits.description: Massassi grow up in a violent and warlike society, so they constantly watch for threats. Once per encounter as a Reaction, a Massassi can gain the benefit of the Uncanny Dodge I and Uncanny Dodge II Talents until the beginning of the Massassi's next turn.

canonicalTraits.name: Weapon Familiarity
canonicalTraits.description: Massassi treat the Massassi Lanvarok as a Simple Weapon (Ranged) instead of an Exotic Weapon (Ranged).

name: Miraluka

structuralTraits.name: Force Sight
structuralTraits.description: A Miraluka is blind but perceives the world through the Force, ignoring concealment from darkness.

structuralTraits.name: Force Sensitivity
structuralTraits.description: A Miraluka gains the Force Sensitivity feat at 1st level.

bonusFeats.condition: A Miraluka who has Use the Force as a Trained Skill

description: Miraluka are remarkably similar to Humans, except for two important and distinctive differences. Miraluka have no eyes, but they have a natural ability to "see" by using The Force. Since they appear Human- including their speech and mannerisms- Miraluka attempt to blend with Humans when traveling throughout the galaxy. Thus, estimating how often they travel or how many live off their home planet of Alpheridies is difficult, but the number is relativity low. To make matters worse, the Sith Lord Darth Nihilus consumed the colony of Katarr after The Jedi Civil War.

canonicalStats.abilityText: All Miraluka receive a +2 bonus to their Intelligence, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Dexterity. Miraluka have keen minds, but their reliance on The Force to perceive their surroundings prevents them from reacting as quickly as most.

canonicalTraits.name: Conditional Bonus Feat
canonicalTraits.description: A Miraluka who has Use the Force as a Trained Skill gains Force Training as a bonus Feat.

canonicalTraits.name: Force Sight
canonicalTraits.description: Although blind, Miraluka can "see" by using The Force. They act normally, without taking any penalties for blindness when in the presence of living creatures, or life of any kind. This trait enables them to make Perception checks, and read and write as a sighted person. They can ignore the effect of darkness as if they had Darkvision. Miraluka cannot, however, detect creatures that are "invisible" to The Force such as the Yuuzhan Vong and the Ysalamiri. These creatures have Total Concealment from Miraluka, as do creatures in areas the dampen or suppress The Force. Additionally, Miraluka ignore all penalties to Perception checks from Cover and Concealment (but not Total Cover or Total Concealment). Miraluka do not increase the DC of Use the Force checks made to Sense Surroundings against targets with Total Cover.

name: Mon Calamari

structuralTraits.name: Amphibious
structuralTraits.description: A Mon Calamari can breathe both air and water.

structuralTraits.name: Tech Savvy
structuralTraits.description: A Mon Calamari gains a +2 species bonus on Mechanics checks.

description: Amphibious land-dwellers, the Mon Calamari share their Outer Rim homeworld with the aquatic Quarren. They tend to be soft-spoken but vigorously defend causes that inspire them. Mon Calamari suffered under great oppression under The Galactic Empire; thus, it was one of the first worlds to declare support for The Rebel Alliance. Mon Calamari are widely recognized for their keen analytical and organizational abilities, and they have developed a reputation as being among the foremost ship designers in the galaxy. They see everything they create as a work of art, not just a tool or weapon.

canonicalStats.abilityText: All Mon Calamari receive +2 bonuses to their Intelligence and Wisdom, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Constitution. Mon Calamari are bright and prudent, but they have relatively frail physiques.

canonicalTraits.name: Breathe Underwater
canonicalTraits.description: As amphibious creatures, Mon Calamari can't drown in water.

canonicalTraits.name: Conditional Bonus Feat
canonicalTraits.description: Keenly perceptive, a Mon Calamari with Perception as a Trained Skill gains Skill Focus (Perception) as a bonus Feat.

canonicalTraits.name: Expert Swimmer
canonicalTraits.description: A Mon Calamari may choose to reroll any Swim check, but the result of the reroll must be accepted, even if it is worse. In addition, a Mon Calamari may choose to Take 10 on Swim checks, even when distracted or threatened.

canonicalTraits.name: Low-Light Vision
canonicalTraits.description: Mon Calamari ignore Concealment (but not Total Concealment) from darkness.

name: Mrlssi

structuralTraits.name: Mercantile Instincts
structuralTraits.description: A Mrlssi gains a +5 species bonus on Persuasion checks made to bargain or negotiate.

description: The diminutive Mrlssi hail from The Colonies region world of Mrlsst, and they are the only sentient Species native to their star system. They evolved from flightless avians, and their faces are dominated by large black eyes and hooked beaks. Mrlssi are typically peaceful and scholarly. Their homeworld has long been home to some of the galaxy's most prestigious universities and research centers, including one of the leading institutions for applied sciences- the Mrlsst Trade and Science Academy. Out of Mrlssi schools grow a host of findings that ease the shipping of food and livestock between worlds, along with innovations that later become standard construction techniques for structures and vessels on planets and in space.

canonicalStats.abilityText: All Mrlssi receive +2 bonuses to their Intelligence and Charisma, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Strength. Mrlssi are small creatures, but they are quick-witted and very charismatic.

canonicalTraits.name: Bonus Class Skill
canonicalTraits.description: Mrlssi have a natural inclination for working with mechanical devices. Mechanics is always a Class Skill for Mrlssi.

canonicalTraits.name: Conditional Bonus Feat
canonicalTraits.description: Mrlssi love intellectual discussions. A Mrlssi with Persuasion as a Trained Skill gains Skill Focus (Persuasion) as a bonus Feat.

canonicalTraits.name: Scholarly Inclination
canonicalTraits.description: At 1st level, a Mrlssi gains any one Knowledge skill as a bonus Trained Skill.

structuralTraits.name: Financial Savant
structuralTraits.description: A Muun gains a +5 species bonus on Persuasion and Knowledge (bureaucracy) checks.

name: Nagai

structuralTraits.name: Cold Adaptation
structuralTraits.description: A Nagai gains a +2 species bonus to Fortitude Defense against cold environments.

description: Honorable, mysterious, violent, and sinister. All these words and more describe the Nagai people. They first appeared to be galactic invaders, until it was revealed that the Nagai were escaping their long-time enemies, the Tofs. Initially hostile to The New Republic, the Nagai eventually formed an alliance of convenience with The New Republic in an attempt to achieve victory in their war against the Tofs. The Nagai are charismatic- a quality that is, by and large, a product of their enhanced vocal range, which allows them to tailor their speech to be more soothing.

canonicalStats.abilityText: All Nagai receive a +2 bonus to both their Dexterity and Charisma, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Constitution. Nagai are charismatic and agile, but physically frail.

canonicalTraits.name: Lightning Reflexes
canonicalTraits.description: Nagai are fast on their feet and gain a +2 Species bonus to their Reflex Defense.

canonicalTraits.name: Soothing Voice
canonicalTraits.description: A Nagai may choose to reroll any Persuasion check, but the result of the reroll must be accepted, even if it is worse.

canonicalTraits.name: Weapon Familiarity
canonicalTraits.description: Nagai treat Tehk'la Blades as Simple Weapons (Melee) instead of as Exotic Weapons (Melee).

name: Nautolan

structuralTraits.name: Aquatic
structuralTraits.description: A Nautolan can breathe water and gains a swim speed equal to its base speed.

structuralTraits.name: Tactile Awareness
structuralTraits.description: A Nautolan gains blindsense out to 6 squares.

description: Nautolans are one of the sentient Species native to the planet Glee Anselm. Their language, Nautila, evolved underwater and is heavily tied to pheromones. Out of water, Nautila loses much of its meaning. As a result, Nautolans learn another language, typically either Basic or Anselmian, the language of the Anselmi people, who are native to Glee Anselm's land masses. Even though they are aquatic, Nautolans have little difficulty living in climates that would be uncomfortable for other amphibians.

canonicalStats.abilityText: All Nautolans receive a +2 bonus to their Constitution, but suffer -2 penalties to both their Intelligence and Wisdom. Nautolan are tough, but also reckless and foolhardy.

canonicalTraits.name: Breathe Underwater
canonicalTraits.description: As amphibious creatures, Nautolans can't drown in water.

canonicalTraits.name: Damage Reduction
canonicalTraits.description: The rubbery hide of a Nautolan provides a Species bonus of Damage Reduction 2.

canonicalTraits.name: Expert Swimmer
canonicalTraits.description: A Nautolan may choose to reroll any Swim check, but the result of the reroll must be accepted, even if it is worse. In addition, a Nautolan may choose to Take 10 on Swim checks, even when distracted or threatened.

canonicalTraits.name: Low-Light Vision
canonicalTraits.description: Nautolans ignore Concealment (but not Total Concealment) from darkness.

canonicalTraits.name: Pheromonal Sensor
canonicalTraits.description: A Nautolan can reroll any Perception check made to Sense Deception or Sense Influence, but the result of the reroll must be accepted, even if it is worse. If underwater, the Nautolan can take the better of the two results instead.

structuralTraits.name: Resilience
structuralTraits.description: Once per encounter, when a Nazren would be moved down the Condition Track by taking damage equal to or exceeding the Nazren's Damage Threshold, the Nazren may reduce the number of steps moved down the Condition Track by 1 (to a minimum of 0 steps).

canonicalStats.abilityText: All Nazren receive +2 bonuses to both their Strength and Constitution, but suffer -2 penalties to their Dexterity and Charisma. Nazren are strong and hardy but taciturn and withdrawn; they shun the spotlight.

canonicalTraits.name: Resilience
canonicalTraits.description: Once per encounter, when a Nazren would be moved down the Condition Track by taking damage equal to or exceeding the Nazren's Damage Threshold, the Nazren may reduce the number of steps moved down the Condition Track by 1 (to a minimum of 0 steps).

name: Neimoidian

structuralTraits.name: Cowardly
structuralTraits.description: A Neimoidian takes a -2 species penalty on Will Defense.

structuralTraits.name: Mercantile Training
structuralTraits.description: A Neimoidian gains a +5 species bonus on Persuasion checks related to business.

description: The Neimoidians quickly find a place in galactic civilization, excited by the prospect of markets beyond their own worlds. For several centuries, they are among the leading forces behind the construction of new starports and the upgrade of existing ones. They trade with everyone- Republic or Sith, Hutt or Herglic- because to the Neimoidians, all that matters is a market for their goods. They help found the Trade Federation, a cartel of mercantile interests that, driven by Neimoidian greed, becomes so powerful that it threatens the very interstellar commerce it exists to protect. When the organization attempts to become the sole conduit for trade in certain parts of space, the Senate and the Jedi intervene.

variants.label: Alternate
variants.description: Alternate Neimoidian trait profile from the canonical species text.

traits.name: Conditional Bonus Feats
traits.description: A Neimoidian with Deception as a Trained Skill gains Skill Focus (Deception) as a bonus Feat.

traits.name: Deceptive
traits.description: A Neimoidians may choose to reroll any Deception check, but the result of the reroll must be accepted, even if it is worse.

canonicalStats.abilityText: Neimoidians receive a +2 bonus to their Charisma, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Strength. Neimoidians avoid physical activity and confrontation, preferring instead to rely on the power of their personalities to achieve success.

canonicalTraits.name: Conditional Bonus Feats
canonicalTraits.description: Neimoidians strive to slant deals in their favor, and they make agreements that they can break easily if they feel so inclined. A Neimoidian with Deception as a Trained Skill gains Skill Focus (Deception) as a bonus Feat. A Neimoidian Trained in Persuasion gains Skill Focus (Persuasion) as a bonus Feat.

name: Nelvaanian

structuralTraits.name: Feral Instincts
structuralTraits.description: A Nelvaanian gains a +2 species bonus on Initiative checks.

description: Nelvaanians, also referred to as Nelvaans, are a Species of tribal humanoids from the planet Nelvaan. They are a primitive Species, using only crude technology. Male Nelvaanians are responsible for hunting game and protecting the tribe, while females are responsible for the day-to-day affairs of their tribe, which includes raising cubs. At the top of the Nelvaanian tribe is the chief, who rules with the help of his mate and a shaman. A tribal chief is not chosen by trial, nor is he elected to his position.

canonicalStats.abilityText: All Nelvaanians receive a +2 bonus to their Wisdom, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Intelligence. Nelvaanians are thoughtful and patient, but are primitive and rooted in tradition.

canonicalTraits.name: Low-Light Vision
canonicalTraits.description: Nelvaanians ignore Concealment (but not Total Concealment) from darkness.

canonicalTraits.name: Primitive
canonicalTraits.description: Nelvaanians do not gain Weapon Proficiency (Heavy Weapons), Weapon Proficiency (Pistols), or Weapon Proficiency (Rifles) as starting Feats at first level, even if their Class normally grants them.

canonicalTraits.name: Scent
canonicalTraits.description: Nelvaanians have a keen sense of smell. At close range (within 10 squares), Nelvaanians ignore Concealment and Cover for purposes of Perception checks, and take no penalty from poor visibility while Tracking.

canonicalTraits.name: Shadow Swiftness
canonicalTraits.description: If a Nelvaanian begins its turn with Concealment from a target or with the target unaware of it, the Nelvaanian does not provoke Attacks of Opportunity when moving through the target's threatened space until the end of the Nelvaanian's turn.

name: Nikto

structuralTraits.name: Hardy Physiology
structuralTraits.description: A Nikto gains a +2 species bonus to Fortitude Defense.

description: Widely recognized as a slave species of the Hutts, the Nikto have served their masters since before the founding of The Old Republic, proving to be cunning warriors in the Hutts' battle with the legendary conqueror Xim the Despot. Wherever the Hutts are, the Nikto are never far behind. Because of their homeworld's radioactive atmosphere, the Nikto have five distinct subspecies, each adapted to a specific type of terrain (see Nikto Survival feat).

canonicalStats.abilityText: All Nikto receive a +2 bonus to their Strength, but suffer -2 penalties to both their Intelligence and Charisma. Nikto are tough and hardened, but their subservient status stunts their willpower and creativity.

canonicalTraits.name: Great Fortitude
canonicalTraits.description: Nikto gain a +2 Species bonus to their Fortitude Defense, having evolved to survive the harsh environment of their homeworld.

canonicalTraits.name: Natural Armor
canonicalTraits.description: A Nikto's tough hide provides a +2 Natural Armor bonus to their Reflex Defense. A Natural Armor bonus stacks with an Armor bonus.

canonicalTraits.name: Survival Instinct
canonicalTraits.description: A Nikto can choose to reroll any Survival check, but the result of the reroll must be accepted, even if it is worse.

name: Noghri

structuralTraits.name: Assassin Training
structuralTraits.description: A Noghri gains a +2 species bonus on Stealth and Initiative checks.

description: The Noghri culture is built around clans (among them Kihm'bar, Bakh'tor, Eikh'mir, and Hakh'khar) with a long history of bloody rivalry. By the time of the Clone Wars, the clans had put aside their differences and learned to coexist, unaware of other intelligent Species in the galaxy. A space battle in orbit over their world led to a starship crash that contaminated the planet with toxic chemicals. The Noghri faced famine and extinction when a black-garbed visitor from the stars arrived: Darth Vader. He promised to save their dying homeworld; in return, the Noghri pledged to serve him and the Empire.

variants.label: Alternate
variants.description: Alternate Noghri trait profile from the canonical species text.

traits.name: Scent
traits.description: Noghri have an extremely strong sense of smell. At close range (within 10 squares), Noghri ignore Concealment and Cover for purposes of Perception checks, and take no penalty from poor visibility while Tracking.

traits.name: Primitive
traits.description: Noghri do not gain Weapon Proficiency (Heavy Weapons), Weapon Proficiency (Pistols), or Weapon Proficiency (Rifles) as starting Feats at first level, even if their Class normally grants them.

traits.name: Bonus Feat
traits.description: All Noghri gain the Martial Arts I feat at 1st level as a bonus Feat.

traits.name: Quick Reflexes
traits.description: A Noghri may choose to reroll any Initiative check, but the result of the reroll must be accepted even if it is worse.

canonicalStats.abilityText: All Noghri receive a +2 bonus to both their Dexterity and Wisdom, but suffer a -4 penalty to their Charisma. Noghri are quick and wise, but serious and not very personable.

canonicalTraits.name: Conditional Bonus Feat
canonicalTraits.description: Noghri are quick and agile fighters. A Noghri gains Running Attack as a bonus Feat at 1st level, provided he or she meets the prerequisites of the Feat (minimum Dexterity of 13).

canonicalTraits.name: Primitive
canonicalTraits.description: Noghri do not gain Weapon Proficiency (Heavy Weapons), Weapon Proficiency (Pistols), or Weapon Proficiency (Rifles) as starting Feats at first level, even if their Class normally grants them.

canonicalTraits.name: Scent
canonicalTraits.description: Noghri have an extremely strong sense of smell. At close range (within 10 squares), Noghri ignore Concealment and Cover for purposes of Perception checks, and take no penalty from poor visibility while Tracking.

name: Nosaurian

structuralTraits.name: Prehensile Tail
structuralTraits.description: A Nosaurian gains a +2 species bonus on Acrobatics checks.

description: The Nosaurians are bipedal reptilian creatures native to New Plympto. They are a proud but embattled Species. A crumbling economy unaided by Republic restrictions forced the Nosaurians to join The Confederacy of Independent Systems. After the defeat of the CIS, the Nosaurians tried to carry on the fight, only to be crushed by Darth Vader's Stormtrooper contingent, the 501st. The Dark Times brought the Nosaurians defeat, enslavement, and the systematic exploitation of their world by their new Imperial masters.

canonicalStats.abilityText: All Nosaurians receive a +2 bonus to their Dexterity, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Charisma. Nosaurian reflexes are quick, but their demeanor is abrasive toward other Species.

canonicalTraits.name: Bonus Class Skill
canonicalTraits.description: A Nosaurian treats the Climb skill as a Class Skill, regardless of Heroic Class.

canonicalTraits.name: Natural Weapons
canonicalTraits.description: A Nosaurians has a Natural Weapon, in the form of sharp horns. When a Nosaurian makes an Unarmed attack, it may choose to use its Natural Weapon, dealing 1d6 points of piercing damage with the attack instead of normal Unarmed damage. Nosaurians are always considered armed with their Natural Weapon.

canonicalTraits.name: Survival Instinct
canonicalTraits.description: A Nosaurian can choose to reroll any Survival check, but the result of the reroll must be accepted, even if it is worse.

name: Nyriaanan

structuralTraits.name: Winged Glide
structuralTraits.description: A Nyriaanan may glide, reducing falling damage to zero if conscious.

description: Nyriaanans are a group of Near-Humans native to the rich mineral world of Nyriaan. Nyriaanans divide themselves along family lines into hundreds of different clans. Some are nomadic, wandering the waterlogged plains of their homeworld, while others inhabit the same groves of Meadlin's Trees that their ancestors settled centuries, or even millennia, before. They are a peaceful people, but the natives are easily angered by offworlders that care more for turning a profit than for honor or integrity. Although they are not technological in nature, Nyriaan's natives foster the technology of their ancestors as well as adopt newer technology as they acquire it.

canonicalStats.abilityText: All Nyriaanans receive a +2 bonus to their Wisdom, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Charisma. Nyriaanans are calm and thoughtful, but rarely interact with outsiders.

canonicalTraits.name: Bonus Class Skill
canonicalTraits.description: Survival is always considered a Class Skill for Nyriaanans.

canonicalTraits.name: Bonus Feat
canonicalTraits.description: Like their Human ancestors, Nyriaanans are versatile and gain one bonus Feat at 1st level.

canonicalTraits.name: Low-Light Vision
canonicalTraits.description: Nyriaanans ignore Concealment (but not Total Concealment) from darkness.

name: O'reenian

structuralTraits.name: Heightened Awareness
structuralTraits.description: An O'reenian may choose to reroll any Perception check, but the result of the reroll must be accepted, even if it is worse.

structuralTraits.name: Persistent
structuralTraits.description: An O'reenian can reroll any Endurance check, but the result of the reroll must be accepted, even if it is worse.

description: The people of O'reen take the defense of their world very seriously. Despite this fact, there are many strange and powerful races throughout The Unknown Regions that have technology that is unfamiliar to the O'reenians, making it difficult for them to sense threats from the outside until it is too late. As a result, the O'reenians have created a network of Orbital Defense Mines that detect, move toward, and attack any Starship approaching O'reen without proper transponder identifiers.

canonicalStats.abilityText: None. O'reenians are trained both in mind and in body, adapting to all circumstances.

canonicalTraits.name: Heightened Awareness
canonicalTraits.description: An O'reenian may choose to reroll any Perception check, but the result of the reroll must be accepted, even if it is worse.

canonicalTraits.name: Persistent
canonicalTraits.description: An O'reenian can reroll any Endurance check, but the result of the reroll must be accepted, even if it is worse.

structuralTraits.name: Auditory Savant
structuralTraits.description: An Ortolan gains a +5 species bonus on Perception checks involving sound.

name: Pa'lowick

structuralTraits.name: Expert Swimmer
structuralTraits.description: A Pa'lowicks may choose to reroll any Swim check, but the result of the reroll must be accepted, even if it is worse. In addition, a Pa'lowicks may choose to Take 10 on Swim checks, even when distracted or threatened.

structuralTraits.name: Hold Breath
structuralTraits.description: Pa'lowicks are at home either in air or in water. A Pa'lowick can hold his or her breath for a number of rounds equal to 25 times his or her Constitution score before needing to make Endurance checks to hold his or her breath.

structuralTraits.name: Primitive
structuralTraits.description: Pa'lowicks do not gain Weapon Proficiency (Heavy Weapons), Weapon Proficiency (Pistols), or Weapon Proficiency (Rifles) as starting Feats at first level, even if their Class normally grants them.

description: A mining expedition discovered the Pa'lowick homeworld about ten years before the Battle of Endor, making Pa'lowicks one of the newer Species to join galactic society. Though technologically primitive, Pa'lowicks have a sophisticated and highly adaptive feudal society (ruled by noble families). They welcome contact with other Species and enjoy trading foodstuffs and primitive wares for useful technological devices they cannot build themselves. Regular trade with other Species has done little to change the way Pa'lowicks live. Although they like to collect high-tech items, most are content to "Nest" on their homeworld, enjoying the simple life of farming, fishing, hunting, and storytelling.

canonicalStats.abilityText: All Pa'lowicks receive a +2 bonus to their Wisdom, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Strength. Pa'lowicks are a contemplative Species, but their lack of muscle mass hampers their physical prowess.

canonicalTraits.name: Expert Swimmer
canonicalTraits.description: A Pa'lowicks may choose to reroll any Swim check, but the result of the reroll must be accepted, even if it is worse. In addition, a Pa'lowicks may choose to Take 10 on Swim checks, even when distracted or threatened.

canonicalTraits.name: Hold Breath
canonicalTraits.description: Pa'lowicks are at home either in air or in water. A Pa'lowick can hold his or her breath for a number of rounds equal to 25 times his or her Constitution score before needing to make Endurance checks to hold his or her breath.

canonicalTraits.name: Primitive
canonicalTraits.description: Pa'lowicks do not gain Weapon Proficiency (Heavy Weapons), Weapon Proficiency (Pistols), or Weapon Proficiency (Rifles) as starting Feats at first level, even if their Class normally grants them.

name: Pau'an

structuralTraits.name: Authority
structuralTraits.description: As a Species accustomed to being in authority, Pau'ans add a +1 bonus to any insight or morale bonuses they grant to themselves or other characters.

structuralTraits.name: Conditional Bonus Feat
structuralTraits.description: A Pau'an with Persuasion as a Trained Skill gains Skill Focus (Persuasion) as a bonus Feat.

structuralTraits.name: Low-Light Vision
structuralTraits.description: Pau'ans ignore Concealment (but not Total Concealment) from darkness.

description: Xenobiologists from the University of Sanbra have uncovered connections between the Pau'ans and the Utai, the smaller Species with whom they share the planet of Utapau. Genetic sampling has confirmed a few tenuous biological links, though the exact evolutionary process by which the Pau'an Species developed separately from the Utai is still a mystery. Xenoanthropologists date the first Pau'an civilizations back almost 57,000 years before the Battle of Yavin, making theirs one of the youngest known civilizations. Though no written history of this time exists, scientists currently theorize that the long-lived Pau'ans are genetically closer to their progenitor Species, and that as a result of their longevity the Pau'ans have likely only evolved a little since the planet was settled. Some time in the last ten thousand years, Utapau underwent a climate change that caused winds on the surface of the world to reach and maintain constant bursts at destructive speeds.

canonicalStats.abilityText: All Pau'ans receive +2 bonuses to both their Wisdom and Charisma, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Strength. Pau'ans leave most of the manual labor to their Utai neighbors, but they are capable leaders.

canonicalTraits.name: Authority
canonicalTraits.description: As a Species accustomed to being in authority, Pau'ans add a +1 bonus to any insight or morale bonuses they grant to themselves or other characters.

canonicalTraits.name: Conditional Bonus Feat
canonicalTraits.description: A Pau'an with Persuasion as a Trained Skill gains Skill Focus (Persuasion) as a bonus Feat.

canonicalTraits.name: Low-Light Vision
canonicalTraits.description: Pau'ans ignore Concealment (but not Total Concealment) from darkness.

name: Phindian

structuralTraits.name: Bonus Feat
structuralTraits.description: All Phindians gain Skill Training (Knowledge (Technology)) as a bonus Feat at 1st level.

structuralTraits.name: Conditional Bonus Feat
structuralTraits.description: Phindians that have Mechanics as a Trained Skill gain Skill Focus (Mechanics) as a bonus Feat.

description: Phindians are technologically advanced sentients known for creating a variety of technical wonders, including devices capable of erasing one's memory. Before the rise of the Empire, a criminal organization called The Syndicat ruled the Phindian homeworld. The Phindian leaders of The Syndicat used their control over the distribution of food and other provisions to keep the general population in line. Any who resisted The Syndicat had their memories erased and were exiled to other worlds. The Jedi helped the Phindian population overthrow The Syndicat and install a democratic government, which remained in place until the formation of the Emperor's New Order.

canonicalStats.abilityText: Phindians receive a +2 bonus to their Intelligence, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Charisma. Phindians are sharp-minded, but typically anti-social.

canonicalTraits.name: Bonus Feat
canonicalTraits.description: All Phindians gain Skill Training (Knowledge (Technology)) as a bonus Feat at 1st level.

canonicalTraits.name: Conditional Bonus Feat
canonicalTraits.description: Phindians that have Mechanics as a Trained Skill gain Skill Focus (Mechanics) as a bonus Feat.

name: Polis Massan

structuralTraits.name: Conditional Bonus Feat
structuralTraits.description: Polis Massans are known across the galaxy for their skill with medicine. A Polis Massan with Treat Injury as a Trained Skill gains Skill Focus (Treat Injury) as a bonus Feat.

structuralTraits.name: Darkvision
structuralTraits.description: Polis Massans ignore Concealment (including Total Concealment) from darkness. However, they cannot perceive colors in total darkness.

structuralTraits.name: Limited Telepathy
structuralTraits.description: In conjunction with their sign language and technological methods of communication, Polis Massans are able to employ a weak form Telepathy that allows them to convey simple feelings and thoughts to any willing recipient within 10 squares. Polis Massans can use the Telepathy aspect of the Use the Force skill Untrained (even if not Force-sensitive). If the target of the Telepathy is within 10 squares, no skill check is required to initiate the telepathic contact.

structuralTraits.name: Mute
structuralTraits.description: Polis Massans have no vocal cords, and must rely on a combination of sign language, computers, and Telepathy in order to communicate with others. When they learn new Languages, they can understand but not speak them.

description: The small asteroid mining colony of Polis Massa in The Outer Rim is the current homeworld of the Polis Massans. Though the Polis Massans evolved on another world in the Subterrel sector, they have been away from their homeworld for so long that they have no living memory of their origins. Instead, they have dedicated themselves to researching a lost civilization, once native to Polis Massa, known as the Eellayin. The planet once inhabited by the Eellayin was subject to a great and mysterious cataclysm which caused it to break apart into several million fragments. The Polis Massa asteroid is one of the largest fragments to survive, and many artifacts and traces of the fallen civilization are hidden within its depths.

canonicalStats.abilityText: All Polis Massans receive a +2 bonus to their Wisdom, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Strength. Polis Massans are thoughtful and ponderous, but their physique is largely lackluster.

canonicalTraits.name: Conditional Bonus Feat
canonicalTraits.description: Polis Massans are known across the galaxy for their skill with medicine. A Polis Massan with Treat Injury as a Trained Skill gains Skill Focus (Treat Injury) as a bonus Feat.

canonicalTraits.name: Darkvision
canonicalTraits.description: Polis Massans ignore Concealment (including Total Concealment) from darkness. However, they cannot perceive colors in total darkness.

canonicalTraits.name: Limited Telepathy
canonicalTraits.description: In conjunction with their sign language and technological methods of communication, Polis Massans are able to employ a weak form Telepathy that allows them to convey simple feelings and thoughts to any willing recipient within 10 squares. Polis Massans can use the Telepathy aspect of the Use the Force skill Untrained (even if not Force-sensitive). If the target of the Telepathy is within 10 squares, no skill check is required to initiate the telepathic contact.

canonicalTraits.name: Mute
canonicalTraits.description: Polis Massans have no vocal cords, and must rely on a combination of sign language, computers, and Telepathy in order to communicate with others. When they learn new Languages, they can understand but not speak them.

name: Quarren

structuralTraits.name: Amphibious
structuralTraits.description: A Quarren can breathe both air and water.

structuralTraits.name: Forceful Presence
structuralTraits.description: A Quarren gains a +2 species bonus on Intimidate checks.

description: The Quarren hail from the distant Outer Rim planet of Mon Calamari, sharing the world with the sentient humanoid Species of the same name. The Mon Calamari live on the surface of the world, while the more isolationist Quarren dwell in oxygen-filled cities in the deep recesses of the oceans. Offworld, Quarren generally stay clear of galactic politics. Instead, they often become involved with shadowy occupations such as pirates, smugglers, and spy networks. Many Quarren blame both the Empire and the Rebels (even more than the Mon Calamari) for their homeworld's devastation during the Galactic Civil War.

canonicalStats.abilityText: Quarren receive a +2 bonus to their Constitution, but take -2 penalties to both Wisdom and Charisma. Quarren are extremely resilient and physically tough, though they tend toward deficiencies in wisdom and social graces.

canonicalTraits.name: Breathe Underwater
canonicalTraits.description: As amphibious creatures, Quarren can't drown in water.

canonicalTraits.name: Conditional Bonus Feat
canonicalTraits.description: Adept traders and negotiators, Quarren with Persuasion as a Trained Skill gains Skill Focus (Persuasion) as a bonus Feat.

canonicalTraits.name: Expert Swimmer
canonicalTraits.description: A Quarren may choose to reroll any Swim check, but the result of the reroll must be accepted, even if it is worse. In addition, a Quarren may choose to Take 10 on Swim checks, even when distracted or threatened.

canonicalTraits.name: Low-Light Vision
canonicalTraits.description: Quarren ignore Concealment (but not Total Concealment) from darkness.

name: Rakata

structuralTraits.name: Dark Side Mastery
structuralTraits.description: A Rakata gains a +2 species bonus on Use the Force checks and treats the Use the Force skill as trained.

description: The Rakata and their planet became lost to history, perhaps purposefully erased by the newly freed societies. Their former subjects eliminated their former masters, or forced them to leave. Rakata civilization on their homeworld of Lehon deteriorated until all that remained were small, primitive tribes. Eventually, the Rakata are rediscovered by Darth Revan, who learns the secret of The Star Forge and uses it to build a massive military force to attack the Republic. Darth Revan and the Republic are also responsible for the destruction of The Star Forge.

canonicalStats.abilityText: All Rakata receive a +2 bonus to their Intelligence, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Wisdom. Rakata are crafty and intelligent, but their pride can blind them to their mistakes.

canonicalTraits.name: Ancient Knowledge
canonicalTraits.description: Rakata retain many of the qualities that led their ancestors to create great technological works in the distant past. Rakata reduce the penalty for not being proficient in a weapon from -5 to -2, and reduce the Armor Check Penalty for Armor they are not proficient in by one step (-5 for Heavy Armor, -2 for Medium Armor, and no penalty for Light Armor). All other penalties for not being proficient still apply. Additionally, once per encounter the Rakata can use a Trained-only application of the Mechanics, Pilot, Treat Injury, or Use Computer skills, even if Untrained in that skill.

canonicalTraits.name: Conditional Bonus Feat
canonicalTraits.description: Rakata with Weapon Proficiency (Simple Weapons) or proficient with any Exotic Weapon (Melee) gain the Weapon Focus feat (for Simple Weapons or a chosen Exotic Weapon (Melee)) as a bonus Feat.

canonicalTraits.name: Force Blind
canonicalTraits.description: For reasons unknown, the Rakata have lost their connection to The Force. Rakata cannot take the Force Sensitivity feat. They can still increase their Dark Side Score and are still affected by Force Powers, Force Talents, and the Use the Force skill as normal.

canonicalTraits.name: Primitive
canonicalTraits.description: Rakata do not gain Weapon Proficiency (Heavy Weapons), Weapon Proficiency (Pistols), or Weapon Proficiency (Rifles) as starting Feats at first level, even if their Class normally grants them.

canonicalTraits.name: Rage
canonicalTraits.description: Once per day, a Rakata can fly into a Rage as a Swift Action. While raging, the Rakata temporarily gains a +2 Rage bonus on melee attack rolls and melee damage rolls, but cannot use Skills that require patience and concentration, such as Mechanics, Stealth, or Use the Force. A fit of Rage lasts for a number of rounds equal to 5 + the Rakata's Constitution modifier. At the end of its rage, the Rakata moves -1 Persistent step along the Condition Track. The penalties imposed by this Persistent Condition persist until the Rakata takes at least 10 minutes to recuperate, during which time the Rakata can't engage in any strenuous activity.

canonicalTraits.name: Weapon Familiarity
canonicalTraits.description: Rakata do not treat Rakatan Weapons as Exotic Weapons, instead treating them as a base item (for instance, a Rakatan Blaster Rifle would still be treated as a Rifle).

name: Replica Droid

structuralTraits.name: Artificial Being
structuralTraits.description: A Replica Droid is immune to poison and disease but cannot benefit from organic healing.

description: These Replica Droids were originally designed by the Alliance to Restore the Republic to infiltrate and replace Imperial personnel, but their funding ultimately ran out, causing them to abandon the project. Ironically enough, The Empire completed and perfected the Replica Droid's design, through they were never put into any major use.

canonicalStats.abilityText: All Replica Droids receive a +2 bonus to both their Strength and Dexterity, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Charisma. Replica Droids are built to be physically strong, but have to work to fit in seamlessly with organic beings. Additionally, like other Droids, Replica Droids have no Constitution score.

canonicalTraits.name: Bonus Equipment
canonicalTraits.description: Replica Droids can be constructed with two of the following Droid Systems installed: Internal Comlink, Darkvision, Diagnostics Package, Improved Sensor Package, Internal Storage (subject to size limitations), Translator Unit (DC 15).

canonicalTraits.name: Conditional Bonus Feat
canonicalTraits.description: Seeking to perfectly imitate organic creatures, a Replica Droid that has Deception as a Trained Skill gains Skill Focus (Deception) as a bonus Feat.

canonicalTraits.name: Droid Traits
canonicalTraits.description: Replica Droids operate as if they are 4th-Degree Droids, except that they ignore the Maintenance requirement. Replica Droids have all Droid Immunities and normal Droid Traits.

name: Republic Clone

structuralTraits.name: Bonus Feat
structuralTraits.description: Republic Clones gain one bonus Feat at 1st level.

structuralTraits.name: Bonus Trained Skill
structuralTraits.description: Republic Clones are versatile and accomplished at many tasks. A Republic Clone character chooses one additional Trained Skill at 1st level. The Skill must be chosen from the character's list of Class Skills.

description: Given the prominence of Clones in the story of the Clone Wars, Gamemasters should not be surprised if players wish to play Clone characters. Clone Trooper heroes can be interesting and present exciting story opportunities, especially for a Gamemaster who plans to use Order 66 in the campaign finale. Gamemasters should allow players to play Clone Trooper heroes who stand out from the rank-and-file Clone Troopers. The basic Republic Clone is bred for obedience and military discipline. This can stifle players who want more free will.

canonicalStats.abilities: Republic Clones begin play with the following Ability Score array: Strength 15, Dexterity 13, Constitution 10, Intelligence 12, Wisdom 10, and Charisma 8. Additionally, a Republic Clone player can choose one Ability Score to increase by 2 at the time of Character Creation.
canonicalStats.abilityText: Republic Clones begin play with the following Ability Score array: Strength 15, Dexterity 13, Constitution 10, Intelligence 12, Wisdom 10, and Charisma 8. Additionally, a Republic Clone player can choose one Ability Score to increase by 2 at the time of Character Creation.

abilityChoice.label: Republic Clone Training Emphasis
abilityChoice.description: Republic Clones begin play with the following Ability Score array: Strength 15, Dexterity 13, Constitution 10, Intelligence 12, Wisdom 10, and Charisma 8. Additionally, a Republic Clone player can choose one Ability Score to increase by 2 at the time of Character Creation.
abilityChoice.helpText: Republic Clones start with STR 15, DEX 13, CON 10, INT 12, WIS 10, CHA 8, then distribute two +1 attribute increases as if using the level 4 attribute increase step.

canonicalTraits.name: Bonus Feat
canonicalTraits.description: Republic Clones gain one bonus Feat at 1st level.

canonicalTraits.name: Bonus Trained Skill
canonicalTraits.description: Republic Clones are versatile and accomplished at many tasks. A Republic Clone character chooses one additional Trained Skill at 1st level. The Skill must be chosen from the character's list of Class Skills.

name: Rodian

structuralTraits.name: Keen Senses
structuralTraits.description: A Rodian gains a +2 species bonus on Perception checks.

description: Rodians hail from the Tyrius System of The Mid Rim, their homeworld humid and choked with heavy rain forests teeming with dangerous life forms. In this hostile environment, the Rodians evolved into brutal hunters and killers to survive. As a result, Rodian culture centers around the concept of "The Hunt." Their art glorifies violence and the art of stalking prey. The more intelligent and dangerous a hunter's prey, the more honorable the hunt. Rodians have numerous annual festivals that exists solely to honor such activities.

canonicalStats.abilityText: All Rodians receive a +2 bonus to Dexterity, but they suffer -2 penalties to both their Wisdom and Charisma. Rodians are quick and well coordinated, but often act before thinking things through, and are often rude and try to bully others.

canonicalTraits.name: Conditional Bonus Feat
canonicalTraits.description: Rodians are taught how to track and survive in the wilderness of Rodia from a very young age. A Rodian with Survival as a Trained Skill gains Skill Focus (Survival) as a bonus Feat.

canonicalTraits.name: Heightened Awareness
canonicalTraits.description: A Rodian may choose to reroll any Perception check, but the result of the reroll must be accepted, even if it is worse.

canonicalTraits.name: Low-Light Vision
canonicalTraits.description: Rodians ignore Concealment (but not Total Concealment) from darkness.

name: Ryn

structuralTraits.name: Natural Performer
structuralTraits.description: A Ryn gains a +5 species bonus on Perform checks.

description: Ubiquitous in spaceports throughout the galaxy, Ryn are a Species of nomads and adventurers, scoundrels, strels, and rogues. No one is certain where the Ryn come from; no record of a homeworld exists, and Ryn culture, such as it is, requires them to never sleep in the same place twice. Some speculate that the Ryn were a race of warriors, dispatched to Core Worlds ages ago to contend with some threat, while others suggest that the Ryn were a tribe of ten thousand musicians gifted to a world that had no artists of their own. Either could be true, for the Ryn are as fearless in battle as they are skilled in music. For all their talents, Ryn find the galaxy largely inhospitable.

canonicalStats.abilityText: All Ryn receive a +2 bonus to their Charisma. Ryn are gifted entertainers and performers.

canonicalTraits.name: Connections
canonicalTraits.description: Ryn benefit from an extensive information network that spans the galaxy. Whenever they travel, they can contact other Ryn to learn rumors and guarded information more easily than other races. Whenever a Ryn makes a Gather Information check to Learn Secret Information, they can forgo the credit cost. If this check fails, the Ryn can try again, but must spend the credits as normal.

canonicalTraits.name: Musical Obfuscation
canonicalTraits.description: Ryn can move air through their beaks to create pleasant tunes and notes. A Ryn can reroll any Deception check to convey Deceptive Information, keeping the better of the two results. A Ryn can project their sounds out to a range of 10 squares. As a Swift Action, a Ryn can Create a Diversion to Hide.

canonicalTraits.name: Prehensile Tail
canonicalTraits.description: Ryn have long tails nearly as strong and flexible as their hands. A Ryn can effectively hold (but not wield) a Small or smaller item with its tail. When holding a Medium or larger item with its tail, the Ryn is Encumbered and considered to be carrying a Heavy Load.

name: Sakiyan

structuralTraits.name: Darkvision
structuralTraits.description: Sakiyans ignore Concealment (including Total Concealment) from darkness. However, they cannot perceive colors in total darkness.

structuralTraits.name: Conditional Bonus Feat
structuralTraits.description: A Sakiyan with Perception as a Trained Skill gains Skill Focus (Perception) as a bonus Feat.

structuralTraits.name: Lightning Reflexes
structuralTraits.description: Sakiyans gain a +2 Species bonus to their Reflex Defense.

conditionalTraits.name: Aerial Hunter
conditionalTraits.description: A Sakiyan gains a +2 species bonus on Initiative checks while flying.

description: Sakiyans rarely venture far from their home system, showing little interest in traveling or trading with the galaxy at large. During the days of the Old Republic, there was some dispute among xenobiologists as to whether the Sakiyans should be classified as a Near-Human Species. The unusual structure of their brain led Imperial xenobiologists to conclude that they were a distinctly alien Species. The highly evolved Sakiyan brain is nearly twice the size of the average Human brain. While the Bith brain remains one of the most advanced among common alien Species, the parts of the Sakiyan brain that process sensory input are superior even to the Bith.

canonicalStats.abilityText: Sakiyans are notable versatile for a Species, and as such receive no bonuses or penalties to their Ability Scores.

canonicalTraits.name: Darkvision
canonicalTraits.description: Sakiyans ignore Concealment (including Total Concealment) from darkness. However, they cannot perceive colors in total darkness.

canonicalTraits.name: Conditional Bonus Feat
canonicalTraits.description: A Sakiyan with Perception as a Trained Skill gains Skill Focus (Perception) as a bonus Feat.

canonicalTraits.name: Lightning Reflexes
canonicalTraits.description: Sakiyans gain a +2 Species bonus to their Reflex Defense.

name: Selkath

structuralTraits.name: Amphibious
structuralTraits.description: A Selkath can breathe both air and water.

structuralTraits.name: Aquatic Adaptation
structuralTraits.description: A Selkath gains a +2 species bonus to Fortitude Defense.

description: Few Selkath travel offworld, preferring the habitat of their homeworld. They have never joined the Republic, or any galactic organization, so most have little reason to leave. They enjoy the technology and wealth produced by the sale of Kolto. All contact with offworlders occurs in Ahto; the Selkath live in underwater cities and settlements scattered around the planet. As amphibious creatures, they are equally at home in either locale.

canonicalStats.abilityText: All Selkath receive a +2 bonus to their Charisma. Most Selkath tend to be skilled in talking their way out of an argument, without taking a side.

canonicalTraits.name: Able Healers
canonicalTraits.description: Whenever a Selkath restores Hit Points to another character by any means, or Aids Another who is doing so, the target regains an additional 5 Hit Points. Additionally, once per encounter as a Swift Action, the Selkath can grant an adjacent ally Bonus Hit Points equal to 10 + the Selkath's Character Level, which last until the end of the encounter.

canonicalTraits.name: Breathe Underwater
canonicalTraits.description: As amphibious creatures, Selkath can't drown in water.

canonicalTraits.name: Expert Swimmer
canonicalTraits.description: A Selkath may choose to reroll any Swim check, but the result of the reroll must be accepted, even if it is worse. In addition, a Selkath may choose to Take 10 on Swim checks, even when distracted or threatened.

name: Shard

structuralTraits.name: Crystalline Body
structuralTraits.description: A Shard is immune to poison and disease and does not need to breathe.

description: Shards are crystalline beings native to the planet Orax. Although immobile and silicone-based, Shards are sentient and can communicate with others through computers and other electronic receivers. Some Shards, like The Iron Knights, have themselves placed inside Droid Bodies that they can manipulate telepathically. Many, if not all, Shards are Force-sensitive. Shards in Droid Bodies, disconnected from their group-mind, became insatiably curious, and almost intoxicated by the new sensory experiences.

canonicalStats.abilityText: All Shards receive a +2 bonus to their Wisdom, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Dexterity. Shards have powerful minds, but must rely on somewhat clumsy Droid Bodies. A Shard retains its Constitution score, even when inside a Droid Body.

canonicalTraits.name: Bonus Trained Skill
canonicalTraits.description: Shards gain Use Computer as a bonus Trained Skill, regardless of their Class.

canonicalTraits.name: Droid Shell
canonicalTraits.description: Shards must inhabit Droid Bodies to travel and function in the galaxy at large. In this form, they gain the following traits: Shards must undergo Maintenance, as per a Droid. Shards are immune to Poison, Radiation, noncorrosive Atmospheric Hazards, and Vacuum. They can regain Hit Points only through the Repair Droid application of the Mechanics skill. They do not benefit from Natural Healing, unless they have the Force Repair Talent. Shards are considered cybernetic creatures for the purposes of Ion Damage. Shards can make use of Droid systems and accessories, subject to some limitations. Shards can have the following Droid Systems, with a Cost Modifier of x1: Appendages Droid Armor Communications Systems Compartment Space Sensor Systems Translator Units

name: Shistavanen

structuralTraits.name: Bonus Class Skill
structuralTraits.description: Survival is always a Class Skill for a Shistavanen.

structuralTraits.name: Conditional Bonus Feat
structuralTraits.description: A Shistavanen Trained in Perception gains Skill Focus (Perception) as a bonus Feat.

structuralTraits.name: Low-Light Vision
structuralTraits.description: Shistavanen ignore Concealment (but not Total Concealment) from darkness.

structuralTraits.name: Natural Weapons
structuralTraits.description: A Shistavanen has Natural Weapons, in the form of sharp claws. When a Shistavanen makes an Unarmed attack, it may choose to use its Natural Weapons, dealing 1d6 points of Slashing damage with the attack instead of normal Unarmed damage. A Shistavanen is always considered armed with their Natural Weapons.

structuralTraits.name: Quick Reflexes
structuralTraits.description: A Shistavanen may choose to reroll any Initiative check, but the result of the reroll must be accepted even if it is worse.

description: More commonly known to the galactic populace by the nickname Wolfmen, the Shistavanen are an isolationist Species that originates in the Uvena System. Having evolved from predators, Shistavanen excel at hunting and can track prey through crowded urban streets and across desolate plains with equal ease. Although the vast majority of Shistavanen prefer to remain within their home system, a growing minority choose to travel the galaxy's Hyperlanes. Even during the height of the First Galactic Empire's discrimination against non-Humans, Imperial Intelligence made frequent use of those Shistavanen it could recruit; other Shistavanen joined The Rebel Alliance.

canonicalStats.abilityText: All Shistavanen receive a +2 bonus to their Dexterity, but suffer -2 penalties to both their Intelligence and Charisma. Shistavanen are lithe and agile, but their gruff demeanor isolates them from other Species.

canonicalTraits.name: Bonus Class Skill
canonicalTraits.description: Survival is always a Class Skill for a Shistavanen.

canonicalTraits.name: Conditional Bonus Feat
canonicalTraits.description: A Shistavanen Trained in Perception gains Skill Focus (Perception) as a bonus Feat.

canonicalTraits.name: Low-Light Vision
canonicalTraits.description: Shistavanen ignore Concealment (but not Total Concealment) from darkness.

canonicalTraits.name: Natural Weapons
canonicalTraits.description: A Shistavanen has Natural Weapons, in the form of sharp claws. When a Shistavanen makes an Unarmed attack, it may choose to use its Natural Weapons, dealing 1d6 points of Slashing damage with the attack instead of normal Unarmed damage. A Shistavanen is always considered armed with their Natural Weapons.

canonicalTraits.name: Quick Reflexes
canonicalTraits.description: A Shistavanen may choose to reroll any Initiative check, but the result of the reroll must be accepted even if it is worse.

name: Skakoan

structuralTraits.name: Bonus Feat
structuralTraits.description: Skakoans receive the Armor Proficiency (Light) feat at 1st level as a bonus Feat.

structuralTraits.name: Conditional Bonus Feat
structuralTraits.description: Skakoans with Mechanics as a Trained Skill gain Skill Focus (Mechanics) as a bonus Feat.

structuralTraits.name: Mechanical Savant
structuralTraits.description: Once per encounter, a Skakoan can make a Standard Action Mechanics or Use Computer check as a Swift Action.

structuralTraits.name: Special Equipment
structuralTraits.description: Skakoans suffer limitations outside their native atmosphere. Without a Pressure Suit, a Skakoan takes 1d6 points of damage each round and begins to Suffocate. Skakoan characters begin play with a Light Pressure Suit at no cost.

description: Skakoans are a technologically advanced Species that specializes in microelectronics, Starship engineering, and cutting-edge manufacturing techniques. During the time of the Republic, Skakoans were among the most influential Species in the Techno Union, a galaxy-spanning consortium of megacorporations with representation in the Galactic Senate. Despite the misgivings of other high-ranking members of the Techno Union, Skakoan foreman Wat Tambor pledged to support the Separatists under Count Dooku. The Senate responded by expelling their Techno Union representatives. This contributed to the Skakoan homeworld breaking away from the Republic and joining the Separatist Movement.

canonicalStats.abilityText: All Skakoans receive a +2 bonus to their Intelligence, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Dexterity. Skakoans lack agility but have a rigidly logical, almost computerlike mind.

canonicalTraits.name: Bonus Feat
canonicalTraits.description: Skakoans receive the Armor Proficiency (Light) feat at 1st level as a bonus Feat.

canonicalTraits.name: Conditional Bonus Feat
canonicalTraits.description: Skakoans with Mechanics as a Trained Skill gain Skill Focus (Mechanics) as a bonus Feat.

canonicalTraits.name: Mechanical Savant
canonicalTraits.description: Once per encounter, a Skakoan can make a Standard Action Mechanics or Use Computer check as a Swift Action.

canonicalTraits.name: Special Equipment
canonicalTraits.description: Skakoans suffer limitations outside their native atmosphere. Without a Pressure Suit, a Skakoan takes 1d6 points of damage each round and begins to Suffocate. Skakoan characters begin play with a Light Pressure Suit at no cost.

name: Sluissi

structuralTraits.name: Careful Worker
structuralTraits.description: A Sluissi who takes twice the normal amount of time required to use the Mechanics skill gains a +10 circumstance bonus to the Mechanics check.

structuralTraits.name: Conditional Bonus Feat
structuralTraits.description: Sluissi quickly learn to tinker with technology. A Sluissi who has Mechanics as a Trained Skill gains the Tech Specialist Feat as a bonus Feat.

structuralTraits.name: Serpentine
structuralTraits.description: By coiling their serpentine bodies, Sluissi are able to make long jumps without getting a running start. Sluissi are always considered to have a Running Start for Jump checks. They also gain a +5 stability bonus when making Grapple checks against the Trip feat.

description: Sluissi Technicians are renowned for Starship construction and modification. Although Sluissi methods are slower than others, customers are willing to wait to own the best Starship technology available. The Sluissi consider Starship construction to be an art form. So respected are Sluissi shipyards that the slow pace of construction is tolerated by even the Empire, albeit impatiently.

canonicalStats.abilityText: All Sluissi receive a +2 bonus to their Intelligence, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Dexterity. Sluissi are slow and methodical, choosing to take their time with their work.

canonicalTraits.name: Careful Worker
canonicalTraits.description: A Sluissi who takes twice the normal amount of time required to use the Mechanics skill gains a +10 circumstance bonus to the Mechanics check.

canonicalTraits.name: Conditional Bonus Feat
canonicalTraits.description: Sluissi quickly learn to tinker with technology. A Sluissi who has Mechanics as a Trained Skill gains the Tech Specialist Feat as a bonus Feat.

canonicalTraits.name: Serpentine
canonicalTraits.description: By coiling their serpentine bodies, Sluissi are able to make long jumps without getting a running start. Sluissi are always considered to have a Running Start for Jump checks. They also gain a +5 stability bonus when making Grapple checks against the Trip feat.

name: Snivvian

structuralTraits.name: Craven
structuralTraits.description: A Snivvian takes a -2 species penalty to Will Defense.

structuralTraits.name: Opportunistic
structuralTraits.description: A Snivvian gains a +2 species bonus on Deception checks.

description: Historically, Snivvians have withstood numerous hardships that they channel into their impressive artistic works. After gaining the ability to travel between the stars, the Snivvians attracted the brutal attention of The Thalassian Slavers. Snivvian culture was nearly destroyed before the Republic discovered the practice and stepped in to protect the Snivvians. This protection has been reduced during eras of warfare, but combined Republic and Snivvian forces remain sufficient to hold off lesser threats. During The Old Republic Era, the Snivvians were still rebuilding the damage done to their society by the slavers and other threats.

canonicalStats.abilityText: All Snivvians receive a +2 bonus to their Wisdom. Snivvians tend to be thoughtful and observant of their surroundings.

canonicalTraits.name: Cold Resistance
canonicalTraits.description: The Snivvians' thick skin provides protection from extreme cold. Snivvians gain a +5 Species bonus to their Fortitude Defense to resist Extreme Cold.

canonicalTraits.name: Observant
canonicalTraits.description: Snivvians are highly aware of their surroundings, giving them an advantage when dealing with others. Once per encounter, a Snivvian can use their Perception skill modifier in place of their Deception or Persuasion skill modifier.

name: Sorcerer of Rhand

structuralTraits.name: Beastly Traits
structuralTraits.description: A Sorcerer gains two Beastly Traits unique to his or her relationship with The Dark Side. Create these qualities (with approval from your Gamemaster), or select two using the Creature Generator.

structuralTraits.name: Bonus Feat
structuralTraits.description: Sorcerers of Rhand receive the Force Sensitivity feat at 1st level as a bonus Feat.

structuralTraits.name: Dark-Infused
structuralTraits.description: Sorcerers of Rhand gain the Force Training feat as a bonus Feat, but half of the Force Powers selected with this bonus Feat must have the [Dark Side] descriptor, and none can have the [Light Side] descriptor.

structuralTraits.name: Repulsive Appearance
structuralTraits.description: The Sorcerers of Rhand's repulsive physical appearances allow them to reroll Persuasion checks to Intimidate, and may keep the better result. They also gain a +5 bonus to their Will Defense when others attempt to use the Persuasion skill against them.

description: The Sorcerers of Rhand live deep in The Unknown Regions, hidden by the red flow of the Perann Nebula from any disciples of The Dark who wish to tap their knowledge. Their reclusiveness is a blessing, for if the Sorcerers of Rhand were to unleash the full entropic power of what they call The Dark, the entire galaxy might come to ruin. The Sorcerers or Rhand are realists. They understand that the power of destruction, coupled with time, has more ability to change the universe than any effort of creation. They devote their efforts to understanding its purpose and ultimately divining a way to become agents of The Dark.

canonicalStats.abilityText: All Sorcerers of Rhand receive a +4 bonus to their Intelligence, as well as a +2 bonus to their Wisdom. But they suffer -2 penalties to their Strength, Dexterity, and Charisma. Sorcerers lose their physical attributes in exchange for increased mental abilities.

canonicalTraits.name: Beastly Traits
canonicalTraits.description: A Sorcerer gains two Beastly Traits unique to his or her relationship with The Dark Side. Create these qualities (with approval from your Gamemaster), or select two using the Creature Generator.

canonicalTraits.name: Bonus Feat
canonicalTraits.description: Sorcerers of Rhand receive the Force Sensitivity feat at 1st level as a bonus Feat.

canonicalTraits.name: Dark-Infused
canonicalTraits.description: Sorcerers of Rhand gain the Force Training feat as a bonus Feat, but half of the Force Powers selected with this bonus Feat must have the [Dark Side] descriptor, and none can have the [Light Side] descriptor.

canonicalTraits.name: Repulsive Appearance
canonicalTraits.description: The Sorcerers of Rhand's repulsive physical appearances allow them to reroll Persuasion checks to Intimidate, and may keep the better result. They also gain a +5 bonus to their Will Defense when others attempt to use the Persuasion skill against them.

name: Squib

structuralTraits.name: Natural Diplomat
structuralTraits.description: A Squib gains a +5 Species bonus to Persuasion checks made to Haggle. Additionally, Persuasion is always a Class Skill for Squibs.

structuralTraits.name: Tech Savvy
structuralTraits.description: A Squib can reroll any Knowledge (Technology) check, but the result of the reroll must be accepted, even if it is worse. Additionally, a Squib is not limited to Common Knowledge when making an Untrained Knowledge (Technology) check.

structuralTraits.name: Weapon Familiarity
structuralTraits.description: Squibs treat the Squib Tensor Rifle as a Rifle, rather than as an Exotic Weapon (Ranged).

description: Squibs are known throughout the galaxy for their talents at collecting, trading, and haggling. The uneven distribution of natural resources on their homeworld led early Squibs to compulsively collect and stockpile goods, and to develop a maddeningly complex system of barter. But in spite of their penchant for needless complexities when negotiating, Squibs are surprisingly honest in their dealings, following a specific set of rules whenever they bargain with someone. For Squibs, the process of haggling is more important than the end result.

canonicalStats.abilityText: All Squibs receive a +2 bonus to their Dexterity, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Strength. Squibs are flexible and light on their feet, but generally lack physical strength.

canonicalTraits.name: Natural Diplomat
canonicalTraits.description: A Squib gains a +5 Species bonus to Persuasion checks made to Haggle. Additionally, Persuasion is always a Class Skill for Squibs.

canonicalTraits.name: Tech Savvy
