canonicalTraits.description: Squibs treat the Squib Tensor Rifle as a Rifle, rather than as an Exotic Weapon (Ranged).

name: Ssi-Ruuk

structuralTraits.name: Conditional Bonus Feat
structuralTraits.description: Ssi-Ruuk Trained in the Persuasion skill gain Skill Focus (Persuasion) as a bonus Feat.

structuralTraits.name: Force Blind
structuralTraits.description: Ssi-Ruuk cannot take the Force Sensitivity feat, cannot make Use the Force checks, and never gain Force Points. They can still increase their Dark Side Score and are still affected by Force Powers, Force Talents, and the Use the Force skill as normal.

structuralTraits.name: Natural Armor
structuralTraits.description: The naturally tough scales of the Ssi-Ruuk grant them a +2 Natural Armor bonus to their Reflex Defense. A Natural Armor bonus stacks with an Armor bonus.

structuralTraits.name: Silver Tongued
structuralTraits.description: Ssi-Ruuk use their keen sense of smell to detect changes in a target's emotional state. A Ssi-Ruuk may choose to reroll any Persuasion check, keeping the better of the two results.

structuralTraits.name: The Tofs come from the darkest and most obscure portion of The Unknown Regions
structuralTraits.description: the peripheral halo. Far removed from The Chiss Ascendancy, The Vagaari Empire, and The Ssi-Ruuvi Imperium in the galactic disk, the Tofs and others in the satellite galaxy they call Firefist (and named "Companion Besh" by Republic cartographers) have little understanding of, or sympathy for, traditional galactic power plays- with the exception of their aborted invasion shortly after the Battle of Endor. Tof-0 Their culture is intentionally anachronistic. Violently opposed to streamlined functionality, the Tofs have instead embraced elaborate dress and foppish ritual. Their interstellar vessels resemble traditional sailing ships and sport curved hulls and energy- radiating sails. Their royals wear tailored greatcoats and powdered wigs. Their soldiers carry cutlasses and cudgels and collect battle scars. Attire becomes more ornate as Tofs climb the military hierarchies that make up their society. Low-ranking deckhands wear simple cloth shirts, leather vests, and head-scarves. Ship's captains are outfitted with heavy buccaneer's boots, sparkling rings on nearly every finger, and a waist sash that holds a Blaster Pistol and a carved Club. Tofs do not bathe, covering their stink with perfumed bows on their tunics and boots. Males dominate Tof society. Females are seldom seen and generally kept on-planet where they are visited only when Tof vessels return to port. During long star voyages, Tof males exhibit reckless lust for humanoid females and often make unwanted moves on attractive targets (Or prove vulnerable to feminine flattery).

description: The Ssi-Ruuk are warm-blooded reptilian humanoids who command a stellar empire in The Unknown Regions, well beyond the edges of explored space. Before The Rebellion Era, the existence of the Ssi-Ruuk was unknown to the general galactic population. An escalating war with the competing empire of the Chiss forced the Ssi-Ruuk to look toward the galactic Core for "Recruits" for their space fleet. The Ssi-Ruuvi forces consisted mostly of self-propelled Battle Droids powered by the life energies and enslaved consciousness of captured humanoids subjected to a process "Entechment." The Ssi-Ruuk attacked and subjected the citizens of a number of isolated Outer Rim settlements before clashing directly with the Empire's fleet. Rather than bringing the full might of the Imperial Navy to bear on the Ssi-Ruuk invaders, the Emperor negotiated to a secret treaty with them.

canonicalStats.abilityText: All Ssi-Ruuk receive a +2 bonus to their Strength, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Wisdom. Ssi-Ruuk are physically powerful but prone to panic and delay when faced with mortal danger, especially in space or on worlds that have not been consecrated.

canonicalTraits.name: Conditional Bonus Feat
canonicalTraits.description: Ssi-Ruuk Trained in the Persuasion skill gain Skill Focus (Persuasion) as a bonus Feat.

name: Stereb

structuralTraits.name: Conditional Bonus Feat
structuralTraits.description: Stereb are very adept when working with stone. A Stereb who has Mechanics as a Trained Skill gains Skill Focus (Mechanics) as a bonus Feat. However, this bonus applies only when the Stereb character is working with stone.

structuralTraits.name: Darkvision
structuralTraits.description: Stereb ignore Concealment (including Total Concealment) from darkness. However, they cannot perceive colors in total darkness.

structuralTraits.name: Primitive
structuralTraits.description: Stereb do not gain Weapon Proficiency (Heavy Weapons), Weapon Proficiency (Pistols), or Weapon Proficiency (Rifles) as starting Feats at first level, even if their Class normally grants them.

structuralTraits.name: Subterranean
structuralTraits.description: Stereb are adapted to a subterranean life and can Take 10 on Survival checks when in an underground environment, even when distracted or threatened.

structuralTraits.name: Tough
structuralTraits.description: Like the stone they work with, Stereb are physically hardened, gaining a +5 Species bonus to their Damage Threshold and a +2 Species bonus to their Fortitude Defense.

description: Jokingly called "Hairless Wookiees" by the few who have seen them, Stereb have been found on a number of worlds with rocky crusts. Not technologically adept, the Stereb live in harmony with the surfaces of a planet, working elaborate stone passages underground in the belief that they're part of the world's circulatory system.

canonicalStats.abilityText: All Stereb receive a +4 bonus to their Strength and a +2 bonus to their Constitution, but suffer -2 penalties to their Dexterity, Intelligence, and Charisma. Stereb are strong and hardy. They are not aggressive, and they might take time to reach a decision, but once they choose a course of action, they are deliberate and steadfast.

canonicalTraits.name: Conditional Bonus Feat
canonicalTraits.description: Stereb are very adept when working with stone. A Stereb who has Mechanics as a Trained Skill gains Skill Focus (Mechanics) as a bonus Feat. However, this bonus applies only when the Stereb character is working with stone.

canonicalTraits.name: Darkvision
canonicalTraits.description: Stereb ignore Concealment (including Total Concealment) from darkness. However, they cannot perceive colors in total darkness.

canonicalTraits.name: Primitive
canonicalTraits.description: Stereb do not gain Weapon Proficiency (Heavy Weapons), Weapon Proficiency (Pistols), or Weapon Proficiency (Rifles) as starting Feats at first level, even if their Class normally grants them.

canonicalTraits.name: Subterranean
canonicalTraits.description: Stereb are adapted to a subterranean life and can Take 10 on Survival checks when in an underground environment, even when distracted or threatened.

canonicalTraits.name: Tough
canonicalTraits.description: Like the stone they work with, Stereb are physically hardened, gaining a +5 Species bonus to their Damage Threshold and a +2 Species bonus to their Fortitude Defense.

name: Sullustan

structuralTraits.name: Spatial Awareness
structuralTraits.description: A Sullustan gains a +5 species bonus on Pilot checks and Initiative checks.

description: To survive the natural perils of their harsh, volcanic homeworld, the Sullustans evolved in the planet's numerous caves. They prefer to dwell underground, constructing highly advanced cities of such great beauty that wealthy sightseers come from all over the galaxy to visit them. Living underground helped the Sullustans to develop acute senses, and they are renowned for their navigational and piloting skills. This friendly, gregarious species enjoys interacting with unique, unusual, and interesting beings. When Old Republic scouts first visited their homeworld, the Sullustans quickly embraced galactic civilization.

canonicalStats.abilityText: Sullustans receive a +2 bonus to their Dexterity, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Constitution. Sullustans are quick, agile, and good with ranged weapons, but they are not as hardy as other Species.

canonicalTraits.name: Darkvision
canonicalTraits.description: Sullustans ignore Concealment (including Total Concealment) from darkness. However, they cannot perceive colors in total darkness.

canonicalTraits.name: Expert Climber
canonicalTraits.description: Sullustans are adept at climbing in their underground homes. A Sullustan may choose to Take 10 on Climb checks even when distracted or threatened.

canonicalTraits.name: Heightened Awareness
canonicalTraits.description: A Sullustan may choose to reroll any Perception check, but the result of the reroll must be accepted, even if it is worse.

name: Talz

structuralTraits.name: Cold Adaptation
structuralTraits.description: A Talz gains a +5 species bonus to Fortitude Defense against cold environments.

description: The Empire subjugates the Talz very early in The Dark Times. The New Order wasted no time in secretly enslaving the race and forcing them to mine the mineral wealth of Alzoc III. The Talz do not understand the motives of their new masters, but they can do little but comply. Those few Talz who escape must hide themselves along the fringes of the galaxy, lest the Imperials send them back to their homeworld. During this time, almost no one in the galaxy knows that the Talz even exist.

canonicalStats.abilityText: All Talz receive a +2 bonus to their Constitution, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Intelligence. Winters of Alzoc III make the Talz strong and resilient, but they have had little chance to learn and develop intellectually.

canonicalTraits.name: Conditional Bonus Feat
canonicalTraits.description: Talz learn how to use technology very quickly. A Talz who has Mechanics as a Trained Skill gains Skill Focus (Mechanics) as a bonus Feat.

canonicalTraits.name: Darkvision
canonicalTraits.description: Talz ignore Concealment (including Total Concealment) from darkness. However, they cannot perceive colors in total darkness.

canonicalTraits.name: Natural Weapons
canonicalTraits.description: A Talz has Natural Weapons, in the form of sharp claws. When a Talz makes an Unarmed attack, it may choose to use its Natural Weapons, dealing 1d6 points of Slashing damage with the attack instead of normal Unarmed damage. Talz are always considered armed with their Natural Weapons.

name: Taung

structuralTraits.name: Warrior Culture
structuralTraits.description: A Taung gains a +2 species bonus on melee attack rolls.

description: The Taung are warriors first and foremost, having battled the native Human nations for control of Coruscant before being driven off and forced to flee to the Outer Rim world of Roon, where they remain for millennia until the legendary Mandalore the First leads them to conquer another world. Taung rename both the world and themselves in his honor, becoming The Mandalorians. With a clan-based nomadic culture, Taung view battle and war as both a source of personal honor and a way to show pride in their community. Veterans of successful battles are held in high regard, to such an extent that they are the de facto leaders of their communities. Only the leader of all The Mandalorians, known simply as Mandalore, holds greater sway with the clans.

canonicalStats.abilityText: All Taung receive a +2 bonus to their Constitution, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Charisma. Taung are physically resilient, able to endure when others would give up.

canonicalTraits.name: Armor Training
canonicalTraits.description: Taung characters increase the Maximum Dexterity bonus from worn Armor by +1.

canonicalTraits.name: Superior Defenses
canonicalTraits.description: Because they constantly test themselves both physically and mentally in combat well before reaching adulthood, Taung gain a +1 Species bonus to all their Defenses.

canonicalTraits.name: Warrior Culture
canonicalTraits.description: Taung can reroll any Knowledge (Tactics) check, but the result of the reroll must be accepted, even if it is worse.

name: Thakwaash

structuralTraits.name: Dual Brains
structuralTraits.description: A Thakwaash gains a +2 species bonus to Will Defense and Reflex Defense.

description: Possessed of great strength, these large bipeds are best known for their uniquely non-Human mindset. A Thakwaash has multiple separate personalities, each specializing in a certain function or area of expertise. Interacting with Thakwaash can be difficult for other Species, particularly for Humans, who view such behavior among their own kind as a mental disorder. The maximum number of personalities that a single Thakwaash can develop is unknown, as is the means by which such personalities form in the first place. No reliable reports of Thakwaash exist prior to the Empire's defeat at The Forest Moon of Endor.

canonicalStats.abilityText: All Thakwaash receive a +4 bonus to their Strength, but suffer -2 penalties to both their Dexterity and Charisma. Thakwaash have large, powerful builds, but their multiple personalities make relations with other Species difficult.

canonicalTraits.name: Irrepressible Mind
canonicalTraits.description: Once per encounter, as a Free Action at the start of his or her turn, a Thakwaash can negate or remove any Mind-Affecting effects.

canonicalTraits.name: Natural Armor
canonicalTraits.description: Thakwaash gain a +1 Natural Armor bonus to their Reflex Defense. A Natural Armor bonus stacks with an Armor bonus.

name: Tof

structuralTraits.name: Bonus Feat
structuralTraits.description: Tofs are aggressive and chaotic combatants, firing and swinging wildly. A Tof can take either Rapid Shot or Rapid Strike at 1st level as a bonus Feat.

structuralTraits.name: Conditional Bonus Feat
structuralTraits.description: A Tof gains Mighty Swing as a bonus Feat at 1st level, provided he or she meets the prerequisites of the feat (minimum Strength of 13).

structuralTraits.name: Fearless
structuralTraits.description: Tofs are fearless on the battlefield. Tofs gain a +5 Species bonus to their Will Defense to resist Fear effects, as well as against the Intimidate application of the Persuasion skill.

description: The Tofs come from the darkest and most obscure portion of The Unknown Regions: the peripheral halo. Far removed from The Chiss Ascendancy, The Vagaari Empire, and The Ssi-Ruuvi Imperium in the galactic disk, the Tofs and others in the satellite galaxy they call Firefist (and named "Companion Besh" by Republic cartographers) have little understanding of, or sympathy for, traditional galactic power plays- with the exception of their aborted invasion shortly after the Battle of Endor. Tof-0 Their culture is intentionally anachronistic. Violently opposed to streamlined functionality, the Tofs have instead embraced elaborate dress and foppish ritual. Their interstellar vessels resemble traditional sailing ships and sport curved hulls and energy- radiating sails.

canonicalStats.abilityText: All Tofs receive a +2 bonus to their Strength and Charisma, but suffer -2 penalties to both their Dexterity and Wisdom. Tofs are big, strong, and boisterous, but they often make poor choices in the name of sport and adventure.

canonicalTraits.name: Bonus Feat
canonicalTraits.description: Tofs are aggressive and chaotic combatants, firing and swinging wildly. A Tof can take either Rapid Shot or Rapid Strike at 1st level as a bonus Feat.

canonicalTraits.name: Conditional Bonus Feat
canonicalTraits.description: A Tof gains Mighty Swing as a bonus Feat at 1st level, provided he or she meets the prerequisites of the feat (minimum Strength of 13).

canonicalTraits.name: Fearless
canonicalTraits.description: Tofs are fearless on the battlefield. Tofs gain a +5 Species bonus to their Will Defense to resist Fear effects, as well as against the Intimidate application of the Persuasion skill.

name: Togorian

structuralTraits.name: Claws
structuralTraits.description: A Togorian has natural claw attacks that deal 1d6 points of slashing damage.

description: Togorians in a group are led by the largest, most aggressive, and most ruthless individual among them. They have little honor, but prefer fights against other Togorians to be face-to-face combat. Males and females have little contact with each other. The males are nomadic, often using domesticated Mosgoth as mounts. The females live in the cities, tending animals and seeing to the Togorians' limited technological pursuits.

canonicalStats.abilityText: All Togorians receive a +4 bonus to their Strength, but suffer -2 penalties to both their Intelligence and Wisdom. Togorians are strong and tough, but not terribly bright or learned.

canonicalTraits.name: Feline Reflexes
canonicalTraits.description: Togorians gain a +1 Species bonus to their Reflex Defense.

canonicalTraits.name: Low-Light Vision
canonicalTraits.description: Togorians ignore Concealment (but not Total Concealment) from darkness.

canonicalTraits.name: Physically Intimidating
canonicalTraits.description: When using the Persuasion skill to Intimidate, Togorians use their Strength bonus instead of their Charisma bonus when determining the check modifier.

name: Togruta

structuralTraits.name: Spatial Awareness
structuralTraits.description: A Togruta gains blindsense out to 6 squares.

structuralTraits.name: Pack Instincts
structuralTraits.description: A Togruta gains a +2 species bonus on Initiative checks.

description: Togrutas are natives of the planet Shili, a world of dense forests, wild scublands, and ever-growing urban areas. The most striking features of Togruta are their brightly colored horns, head, head-tails, and skin. These patterns serve as a sort of camouflage that helps them stalk prey through the red and white, meter-tall turu-grass of the scrublands. Togrutas are natural pack hunters and work effectively in large groups. Individualism is generally discouraged, but despite this fact, most Togruta leaders gain power and prestige through expressions of individuality.

canonicalStats.abilityText: All Togrutas receive a +2 bonus to their Dexterity, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Constitution. Togrutas are quick on the hunt, but more delicate than some Species.

canonicalTraits.name: Pack Hunter
canonicalTraits.description: Togrutas deal an additional 2 points of damage on melee attacks against opponents they Flank.

canonicalTraits.name: Sneaky
canonicalTraits.description: A Togruta can choose to reroll any Stealth check to Sneak, but the result of the reroll must be accepted, even if it is worse.

canonicalTraits.name: Spatial Awareness
canonicalTraits.description: Togrutas can sense their surroundings using passive echolocation. Togrutas ignore all Cover and Concealment when making Perception checks to Notice Targets within 10 squares.

name: Toydarian

structuralTraits.name: Force Resistance
structuralTraits.description: A Toydarian gains a +5 species bonus to Will Defense against Force powers.

structuralTraits.name: Wings
structuralTraits.description: A Toydarian may fly with a speed of 6 squares (clumsy).

description: Toydarians are small, winged aliens from the planet Toydaria. They are known as shrewd merchants and able con artists, even though many lead reputable lives. Because their homeworld is located within Hutt Space, many Toydarians who leave Toydaria find themselves either unemployed or indentured to Hutt criminal interests. Toydarians easily resist Mind-Affecting Force Powers and are even more resilient to such abilities than their Hutt overlords. Toydarian1 Toydarians have an unsavory reputation, but not all Toydarians are criminals.

canonicalStats.abilityText: All Toydarians receive a +2 bonus to their Wisdom, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Strength. Toydarians are weaker than Humans, but they are shrewd and accustomed to moving quickly to seal the deal.

canonicalTraits.name: Conditional Bonus Feat
canonicalTraits.description: Toydarians that have Deception as a Trained Skill gain the Skill Focus (Deception) feat as a bonus Feat.

canonicalTraits.name: Flight
canonicalTraits.description: Toydarians are capable of flight, but cannot do so if they are carrying a Heavy Load.

canonicalTraits.name: Force Resistance
canonicalTraits.description: Toydarians receive a +5 bonus to their Will Defense against any use of the Use the Force skill.

name: Trandoshan

structuralTraits.name: Claws
structuralTraits.description: A Trandoshan has natural claw attacks that deal 1d6 points of slashing damage.

activatedAbilities.name: Regeneration
activatedAbilities.description: A Trandoshan regains hit points equal to its level once per encounter when reduced below half hit points.

description: The reptilian Trandoshans are known for their great strength, and warlike natures. Many of these beings dedicate themselves to martial training, and some follow the path of the hunter on their native world. A few have even become renowned (or infamous) bounty hunters in galactic society. Trandoshans have a long-standing enmity with Wookiees, and the two Species have fought often over the centuries. Trandoshans (who refer to themselves as "T'doshok") have supersensitive eyes that can see into the infrared range.

canonicalStats.abilityText: All Trandoshans receive a +2 bonus to their Strength, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Dexterity. Trandoshans are powerful but lack agility.

canonicalTraits.name: Bonus Feat
canonicalTraits.description: Rugged and resilient, Trandoshans receive the Toughness feat at 1st level as a bonus Feat.

canonicalTraits.name: Darkvision
canonicalTraits.description: Trandoshans ignore Concealment (including Total Concealment) from darkness. However, they cannot perceive colors in total darkness.

canonicalTraits.name: Limb Regeneration
canonicalTraits.description: A Trandoshan regrows a lost limb in 1d10 days. At the end of that time, all Persistent Conditions associated with the loss of the limb are removed.

canonicalTraits.name: Natural Armor
canonicalTraits.description: Trandoshans have thick scales that provide a +1 Natural Armor bonus to their Reflex Defense. A Natural Armor bonus stacks with an Armor bonus.

name: Trianii

structuralTraits.name: Feline Instincts
structuralTraits.description: A Trianii gains a +2 species bonus on Initiative checks.

description: The Trianii are a feline race of intrepid explorers, but unlike other technologically advanced Species of the galaxy, they express no interest in becoming a part of the greater galactic community. The Trianii use impressive displays of force to drive away interlopers so that they are left alone to explore their region of the galaxy. Their homeworld is located on the edge of the region of space controlled by The Corporate Sector Authority. An attempt by the CSA to expand its area of control into Trianii territories is met with a display of Trianii determination as the Species expel the invaders, proving that the ships and weapons of The Trianii Rangers are as formidable as those of other galactic forces. Trianii society is a matriarchy- partly because females of the Species are taller and slightly stronger than the males- ruled by tribunals of females known as yu'nar.

canonicalStats.abilityText: All Trianii receive a +2 bonus to their Wisdom. Trianii have excellent vision and, because of their curious nature, are very aware of their surroundings.

canonicalTraits.name: Heightened Agility
canonicalTraits.description: A Trianii can reroll any Acrobatics check, but the result of the reroll must be accepted, even if it is worse.

canonicalTraits.name: Natural Weapons
canonicalTraits.description: A Trianii's sharp claws serve as Natural Weapons. When a Trianii makes an Unarmed attack, it may choose to use its Natural Weapons, dealing 1d6 points of slashing damage with the attack instead of normal Unarmed damage. Trianii are always considered armed with their Natural Weapons.

canonicalTraits.name: Prehensile Tail
canonicalTraits.description: A Trianii's long, flexible tail is dexterous and strong. A Trianii's tail can effectively hold (but not wield) a Small or smaller item. When holding a Medium or larger item with his or her tail, a Trianii is encumbered and is considered to be carrying a Heavy Load.

name: Tusken Raider

structuralTraits.name: Sneaky
structuralTraits.description: A Tusken Raider can choose to reroll any Stealth check to sneak, but the result of the reroll must be accepted, even if it is worse.

structuralTraits.name: Survival Instinct
structuralTraits.description: A Tusken Raider may choose to reroll any Survival check, but the result of the reroll must be accepted even if it is worse.

description: Although technologically primitive by galactic standards, Tusken Raiders make good use of whatever technological items they can salvage from slain offworlders or Jawa. Tusken Raiders use Banthas, large herd animals native to Tatooine, as mounts during scouting missions and hunting expeditions. Some Tusken villages also use domesticated Massiffs as watchdogs. Tusken Hunters typically arm themselves with Gaffi Sticks and Slugthrower Rifles.

canonicalStats.abilityText: Tusken Raiders receive a +2 bonus to their Constitution, but suffer -2 penalties to both their Intelligence and Wisdom. Tusken Raiders are hardy and fit, but are simple and quick to anger.

canonicalTraits.name: Sneaky
canonicalTraits.description: A Tusken Raider can choose to reroll any Stealth check to sneak, but the result of the reroll must be accepted, even if it is worse.

canonicalTraits.name: Survival Instinct
canonicalTraits.description: A Tusken Raider may choose to reroll any Survival check, but the result of the reroll must be accepted even if it is worse.

name: Twi'lek

structuralTraits.name: Graceful
structuralTraits.description: A Twi'lek gains a +2 species bonus to Reflex Defense.

structuralTraits.name: Persuasion Training
structuralTraits.description: A Twi'lek gains a +5 species bonus on Persuasion checks.

description: From the dry, rocky world of Ryloth, Twi'leks have made a place for themselves along the Galaxy. These tall, thin humanoids include a variety of distinct subraces, but all are instantly recognizable by the tentacular "Head-Tails" (called Lekku) that protrude from the backs of their heads. Sly, calculating beings, Twi'leks prefer to avoid trouble and stick to the shadows until an opportunity to act without undue danger to themselves presents itself. Their entrepreneurial spirit frequently leads them to positions of influence, and Twi'lek corporate executives and ambassadors are no less common than unscrupulous Twi'lek freighter captains and crime lords.

canonicalStats.abilityText: All Twi'leks receive a +2 bonus to their Charisma, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Wisdom. Twi'leks make convincing diplomats, but are relatively weak-willed.

canonicalTraits.name: Deceptive
canonicalTraits.description: Naturally gifted at manipulation, a Twi'lek may choose to reroll any Deception check, but the result of the reroll must be accepted, even if it is worse.

canonicalTraits.name: Great Fortitude
canonicalTraits.description: Twi'leks gain a +2 Species bonus to their Fortitude Defense. Twi'leks have great health with a natural resistance to toxins and illnesses.

canonicalTraits.name: Low-Light Vision
canonicalTraits.description: Twi'leks ignore Concealment (but not Total Concealment) from darkness.

name: Ubese

structuralTraits.name: Survivalist
structuralTraits.description: An Ubese gains a +2 species bonus on Endurance and Survival checks.

description: Long ago, the Ubese were a peaceful Species, content in the paradise of their garden planets. This changes when they encounter Old Republic scouts who seduce them with the power and might of advanced technology. Obsessed by these wonders, the Ubese devote their resources to the development of these technologies. As they succeed, they became worried about aggression from rival systems, so they develop weapon technology, dipping into dangerous devices banned throughout the galaxy. Fearing these weapons in the hands of a decidedly unstable population, The Old Republic makes a preemptive strike against the Ubese to destroy weapons facilities and halt their aggression.

canonicalStats.abilityText: All Ubese receive a +2 bonus to their Dexterity, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Constitution. Ubese have slight build, and they are quick and agile.

canonicalTraits.name: Aggressive
canonicalTraits.description: If an Ubese hits with a Charge attack, the +2 bonus on attack rolls against the target persists for the duration of the encounter.

canonicalTraits.name: Conditional Bonus Feat
canonicalTraits.description: Ubese do not trust other cultures, so they are more suspicious of aliens. An Ubese with Perception as a Trained Skill gains Skill Focus (Perception) as a bonus Feat.

canonicalTraits.name: Survival Instinct
canonicalTraits.description: Ubese are tough and resilient, capable of withstanding the perils of even hostile worlds. An Ubese can reroll Survival checks, but the result of the reroll must be accepted, even if it is worse.

canonicalTraits.name: Special Equipment
canonicalTraits.description: Ubese cannot tolerate the atmospheres of other worlds and must rely on breath masks to survive. Without a breath mask, an Ubese begins to Suffocate. When not on their homeworld, Ubese wear environmental suits to stave of the Hazards found on other planets as well as to hide their appearance. An environmental suit functions as a Combat Jumpsuit that also grants a +2 Equipment bonus to Fortitude Defense. The suit also includes a voice modulator to emulate the voice of a male or female or to mask the Ubese's gender. An Ubese environmental suit with a breath mask and voice modulator costs 2000 credits, and a year's supply of breathing filters costs 200 credits (50 credits on Ubese). Ubese characters begin play with these items at no cost.

name: Umbaran

structuralTraits.name: Conditional Bonus Feat
structuralTraits.description: Umbarans are born observers, and they enjoy watching events from hidden vantage points. An Umbaran with Gather Information as a Trained Skill gains Skill Focus (Gather Information) as a bonus Feat.

structuralTraits.name: Darkvision
structuralTraits.description: Umbarans ignore Concealment (including Total Concealment) from darkness. However, they cannot perceive colors in total darkness.

structuralTraits.name: Sneaky
structuralTraits.description: An Umbaran can choose to reroll any Stealth check, but the result of the reroll must be accepted, even if it is worse.

description: As Palpatine's New Order becomes increasingly biased toward Humans, Moore vanishes from the Emperor's side and moves into more shadowy roles within his government. From deep within the intelligence apparatus, she and other Umbarans lead efforts to root out anyone who is not unquestionably loyal to Emperor Palpatine, and they answer only to the Emperor. They are so effective that even Emperor Palpatine's most dedicated followers grow to fear them, something that strengthens the antialien sentiment among many of the Emperor's closest advisers. The Umbarans are particularly disliked by officers of Imperial Intelligence, who resent the fact that they are subject to Umbaran scrutiny- a resentment that grows every time the Umbarans unmask a Rebel double agent in their ranks. After the death of Emperor Palpatine and the collapse of his government, the Umbarans find themselves under threat from the Rebels they had hunted and the New Order supporters they had intimidated.

canonicalStats.abilityText: All Umbarans receive a +2 bonus to both their Dexterity and Wisdom, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Constitution. Umbarans are not as sturdy as many Species, but they are naturally stealthy and observant.

canonicalTraits.name: Conditional Bonus Feat
canonicalTraits.description: Umbarans are born observers, and they enjoy watching events from hidden vantage points. An Umbaran with Gather Information as a Trained Skill gains Skill Focus (Gather Information) as a bonus Feat.

canonicalTraits.name: Darkvision
canonicalTraits.description: Umbarans ignore Concealment (including Total Concealment) from darkness. However, they cannot perceive colors in total darkness.

canonicalTraits.name: Sneaky
canonicalTraits.description: An Umbaran can choose to reroll any Stealth check, but the result of the reroll must be accepted, even if it is worse.

variants.label: Alternate
variants.description: Alternate Umbaran trait profile from the canonical species text.

traits.name: Low-Light Vision
traits.description: Umbarans ignore Concealment (but not Total Concealment) from darkness.

traits.name: Conditional Bonus Feat
traits.description: An Umbaran with Persuasion as a Trained Skill gains Skill Focus (Persuasion) as a bonus Feat.

traits.name: Soothing Voice
traits.description: An Umbaran may choose to reroll any Persuasion check, but the result of the reroll must be accepted, even if it is worse.

traits.name: Light Sensitivity
traits.description: An Umbaran exposed to bright light (such as sunlight) is Blinded for 1 round.

name: Utai

structuralTraits.name: Underground Adaptation
structuralTraits.description: An Utai gains darkvision and a +2 species bonus on Perception checks underground.

description: An ancient race of diminutive humanoids evolving on the Outer Rim planet of Utapau, the Utai make their homes in tunnels and caves amid the sinkholes that pockmarked their homeworld, colonizing them long before the surface dwelling Pau'ans were forced to take refuge in the sinkholes after hyperwinds made life on the surface unbearable. Working together, the Utai allowed the Pau'ans to take the lead in all societal matters, although the Utai make up more than seventy percent of the population on Utapau. Introducing the Utai to wind power, the Pau'ans took over governmental work in the sinkholes and relegated the Utai to a labor class. Despite their role as laborers in Utapaun society, the Utai are not mistreated and prefer their worker status. Many Utai work as Varactyl wranglers and Dactillion tamers, as they possess an innate ability to commune with animals.

canonicalStats.abilityText: All Utai receive a +2 bonus to their Constitution, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Charisma. Utai are sturdy and hard-working, but they do not make good leaders.

canonicalTraits.name: Animal Handler
canonicalTraits.description: The Utai are naturally gifted when it comes to dealing with Beasts and wild creatures. An Utai can choose to reroll any Ride check, but the result of the reroll must be accepted, even if it is worse. Additionally, an Utai can make Persuasion checks to change the Attitude of an undomesticated creature that has an Intelligence score of 2 or lower.

canonicalTraits.name: Cooperative
canonicalTraits.description: An Utai automatically succeeds when attempting to Aid Another on a Skill Check, as long as the target is adjacent to, and can hear and understand, the Utai.

canonicalTraits.name: Low-Light Vision
canonicalTraits.description: Utai ignore Concealment (but not Total Concealment) from darkness.

name: Vagaari

structuralTraits.name: Bioknowledge
structuralTraits.description: Vagaari have great working knowledge of biology throughout their sphere of influence in The Unknown Regions. A Vagaari may choose to reroll any Knowledge (Life Science) or Treat Injury check, but the result of the reroll must be accepted, even if it is worse.

structuralTraits.name: Conditional Bonus Feat
structuralTraits.description: Vagaari are accomplished actors and deceivers. A Vagaari that is Trained in the Deception skill gains Skill Focus (Deception) as a bonus Feat.

structuralTraits.name: Frightening Visage
structuralTraits.description: Vagaari spend most of their lives trying to intimidate others by presenting a threatening appearance. A Vagaari may choose to reroll any Persuasion check made to Intimidate, keeping the better of the two results.

description: The Vagaari Empire thrives on cruelty and terror. It is so effective at spreading fear that even other Unknown Regions Threats, such as the Ebruchi and the Rakata, flee from its forces. Arrogant speciesists, the Vagaari believe that all non-Vagaari are idiotic cowards who exist only as resources to be farmed. The Vagaari have separate digestive and respiratory tracts. The upper mouth is used for breathing and speaking, and the lower mouth is used for eating.

canonicalStats.abilityText: All Vagaari receive a +2 bonus to their Strength, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Wisdom. Vagaari are strong, but self-centered and hostile.

canonicalTraits.name: Bioknowledge
canonicalTraits.description: Vagaari have great working knowledge of biology throughout their sphere of influence in The Unknown Regions. A Vagaari may choose to reroll any Knowledge (Life Science) or Treat Injury check, but the result of the reroll must be accepted, even if it is worse.

canonicalTraits.name: Conditional Bonus Feat
canonicalTraits.description: Vagaari are accomplished actors and deceivers. A Vagaari that is Trained in the Deception skill gains Skill Focus (Deception) as a bonus Feat.

canonicalTraits.name: Frightening Visage
canonicalTraits.description: Vagaari spend most of their lives trying to intimidate others by presenting a threatening appearance. A Vagaari may choose to reroll any Persuasion check made to Intimidate, keeping the better of the two results.

name: Vahla

structuralTraits.name: Bonus Feat
structuralTraits.description: Vahla receive the Force Sensitivity feat at 1st level as a bonus Feat.

structuralTraits.name: Dark Side Stigma
structuralTraits.description: Whether they know it or not, each member of the Vahla Species is forever bound to The Dark Side of The Force. Vahla must spend 2 Force Points to reduce their Dark Side Score by 1 (instead of just 1 Force Point).

structuralTraits.name: Heightened Agility
structuralTraits.description: Vahla skeletons are primarily made of cartilage instead of bone, giving them a more flexible physique. A Vahla can choose to reroll any Acrobatics check, but the result of the reroll must be accepted, even if it is worse.

conditionalTraits.name: Dark Side Affinity
conditionalTraits.description: A Vahla gains a +2 species bonus on Use the Force checks when using dark side powers.

description: The Vahla are a nomadic, Near-Human Species who roamed the galaxy in search of their homeworld, and are generally regarded as a naturally bright and shrewd people. Their small economy is fueled by petty theft and limited-scale piracy, and, following the Great Jedi Purge, by bounty hunting. All Vahla are Force-sensitive, and whether aware or not, have ties to The Dark Side of The Force. Most Vahla become members of The Ember of Vahl, a Dark Side cult that worships Vahl, a goddess of fire and destruction. Though not all Vahla are members, they are all expected to follow the orders of a six-priest central coven believed to have been "Chosen" by the goddess.

canonicalStats.abilityText: All Vahla receive a +2 bonus to their Intelligence, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Constitution. The Vahla are naturally bright and shrewd, but they are physically frail.

canonicalTraits.name: Bonus Feat
canonicalTraits.description: Vahla receive the Force Sensitivity feat at 1st level as a bonus Feat.

canonicalTraits.name: Dark Side Stigma
canonicalTraits.description: Whether they know it or not, each member of the Vahla Species is forever bound to The Dark Side of The Force. Vahla must spend 2 Force Points to reduce their Dark Side Score by 1 (instead of just 1 Force Point).

canonicalTraits.name: Heightened Agility
canonicalTraits.description: Vahla skeletons are primarily made of cartilage instead of bone, giving them a more flexible physique. A Vahla can choose to reroll any Acrobatics check, but the result of the reroll must be accepted, even if it is worse.

name: Verpine

structuralTraits.name: Conditional Bonus Feat
structuralTraits.description: A Verpine who has Mechanics as a Trained Skill gains Skill Focus (Mechanics) as a bonus Feat.

structuralTraits.name: Heightened Awareness
structuralTraits.description: A Verpine can choose to reroll any Perception check, but the result of the reroll must be accepted, even if it is worse.

structuralTraits.name: Hive Mind
structuralTraits.description: A Verpine can mentally communicate with any Verpine within a 1-kilometer range.

structuralTraits.name: Natural Armor
structuralTraits.description: A Verpine's carapace provides a +1 Natural Armor bonus to their Reflex Defense. A Natural Armor bonus stacks with an Armor bonus.

structuralTraits.name: Weapon Familiarity
structuralTraits.description: Verpine treat the Verpine Shattergun as a Pistol instead of an Exotic Weapon (Ranged).

description: The Verpine have been spacefarers for all their recorded history, long before they had contact with other Species. Verpine have traveled for so long that the name and location of their homeworld have been lost to antiquity. They are fascinated with technology in all its forms, and consider themselves to be experts in all technological fields. Any device that falls into Verpine hands is prone to being taken apart, reassembled, and improved with astounding speed and skill. The asteroid field that the Verpine have transformed into a thriving colony is a testament to their mastery of technology.

canonicalStats.abilityText: Verpine are notably versatile for a Species, and as such receive no bonuses or penalties to their Ability Scores.

canonicalTraits.name: Conditional Bonus Feat
canonicalTraits.description: A Verpine who has Mechanics as a Trained Skill gains Skill Focus (Mechanics) as a bonus Feat.

canonicalTraits.name: Heightened Awareness
canonicalTraits.description: A Verpine can choose to reroll any Perception check, but the result of the reroll must be accepted, even if it is worse.

canonicalTraits.name: Hive Mind
canonicalTraits.description: A Verpine can mentally communicate with any Verpine within a 1-kilometer range.

canonicalTraits.name: Natural Armor
canonicalTraits.description: A Verpine's carapace provides a +1 Natural Armor bonus to their Reflex Defense. A Natural Armor bonus stacks with an Armor bonus.

canonicalTraits.name: Weapon Familiarity
canonicalTraits.description: Verpine treat the Verpine Shattergun as a Pistol instead of an Exotic Weapon (Ranged).

name: Vultan

structuralTraits.name: Bonus Trained Skill
structuralTraits.description: A Vultan can choose one additional Trained Skill at 1st level. The Skill must be chosen from the Vultan's list of Class Skills.

structuralTraits.name: Conditional Bonus Feat
structuralTraits.description: A Vultan with one or more Knowledge skills as Trained Skills gains a bonus Skill Focus feat for one Knowledge skill in which they are Trained.

description: Vultans are a technologically advanced Near-Human Species native to the planet Vulta, located at the spinward edge of The Mid Rim. The Vultan culture was already five millennia old at the time of the Great Hyperspace War. As citizens of an old and curious culture, Vultans tend to go through waves of fads and technological growth. Many Old Republic corporations have a few Vultans on their board of directors. Even during the time of the Empire, Vultans commonly worked as programmers for holovid entertainment companies.

canonicalStats.abilityText: All Vultans receive a +2 bonus to their Intelligence, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Wisdom. Vultans are creative and inquisitive, but their whimsical tendencies can often get the better of them.

canonicalTraits.name: Bonus Trained Skill
canonicalTraits.description: A Vultan can choose one additional Trained Skill at 1st level. The Skill must be chosen from the Vultan's list of Class Skills.

canonicalTraits.name: Conditional Bonus Feat
canonicalTraits.description: A Vultan with one or more Knowledge skills as Trained Skills gains a bonus Skill Focus feat for one Knowledge skill in which they are Trained.

name: Vurk

structuralTraits.name: Powerful Swimmer
structuralTraits.description: A Vurk gains a swim speed equal to its base speed and a +2 species bonus on Athletics checks while swimming.

description: Originally considered a primitive Species by galactic survey teams, Vurks are intelligent and philosophically advanced. From birth, they are taught to honor personal integrity, individual freedom, and honesty. Because of these tenants, Vurks make excellent diplomats and negotiators.

canonicalStats.abilityText: All Vurks receive +2 bonuses to both their Constitution and Charisma, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Dexterity. Vurks are resilient and reliable, but their thick fingers lack fine motor movement.

canonicalTraits.name: Breathe Underwater
canonicalTraits.description: As amphibious creatures, Vurks can't drown in water.

canonicalTraits.name: Expert Swimmer
canonicalTraits.description: A Vurk may choose to reroll any Swim check, but the result of the reroll must be accepted, even if it is worse. In addition, a Vurk may choose to Take 10 on Swim checks, even when distracted or threatened.

canonicalTraits.name: Placid
canonicalTraits.description: Vurks are renowned for their ability to remain calm and collected, even in the face of danger. Once per encounter, as a Free Action, a Vurk can remove one Fear effect currently affecting it.

canonicalTraits.name: Survival Instinct
canonicalTraits.description: As nomads, Vurks are accustomed to moving around, even through dangerous places. A Vurk may choose to reroll any Survival check, but the result of the reroll must be accepted even if it is worse.

name: Weequay

structuralTraits.name: Stoic Resolve
structuralTraits.description: A Weequay gains a +2 species bonus to Will Defense.

description: The Weequay are well-known throughout the galaxy for their violent tendencies and lack of individuality. Deeply spiritual, they worship a broad pantheon of deities, the most senior of which is known as Quay. Zealous Weequay perform ritual sacrifices in Quay's honor, at times resorting to murder. Such incidents have done little to dispel their violent reputation. Each Weequay carries a spherical totem through which Quay allegedly speaks.

canonicalStats.abilityText: Weequay receive a +2 bonus to their Constitution, but suffer -2 penalties to both their Intelligence and Charisma. Weequay are resilient but not particularly bright, and they don't relate well to others.

canonicalTraits.name: Conditional Bonus Feat
canonicalTraits.description: Weequay have learned to survive the harsh conditions on Sriluur. A Weequay with Endurance as a Trained Skill gains Skill Focus (Endurance) as a bonus Feat.

canonicalTraits.name: Expressive Pheromones
canonicalTraits.description: Weequay communicate silently with other members of their tribe by emitting complex pheromones. This form of communication has a maximum range of 20 squares. Species with the Scent Species Trait can detect Weequay pheromones by smell, but this does not allow them to understand what the Weequay are communicating.

canonicalTraits.name: Natural Armor
canonicalTraits.description: Weequay posses a thick, leathery hide that provides a +1 Natural Armor bonus to his or her Reflex Defense. A Natural Armor bonus stacks with an Armor bonus.

name: Whiphid

structuralTraits.name: Cold Resistance
structuralTraits.description: Their thick fur gives allows Whiphids to add a +5 Species bonus to their Fortitude Defense to resist Extreme Cold.

structuralTraits.name: Natural Weapons
structuralTraits.description: A Whiphid has Natural Weapons, in the form of sharp claws. When a Whiphid makes an Unarmed attack, it may choose to use its Natural Weapons, dealing 1d6 points of slashing damage with that attack, instead of normal Unarmed damage. Whiphid are always considered armed with their Natural Weapons.

structuralTraits.name: Scent
structuralTraits.description: Whiphids have a keen sense of smell. At close range (within 10 squares), Whiphids ignore Concealment and Cover for purposes of Perception checks, and take no penalty from poor visibility while Tracking.

conditionalTraits.name: Arctic Hunter
conditionalTraits.description: A Whiphid gains a +2 species bonus on Stealth and Survival checks in cold environments.

description: Whiphids are nomadic hunters, living on and making seasonal migrations across the vast tundra of their homeworld. Whiphid society is primitive and tribal, with each tribe led by a Spearmaster. They live for the hunt and the thrill of the kill. When they were discovered by traders during The Old Republic Era, the Whiphids were quite taken with the traders' technology. Since that time, numerous Whiphids have left their tribes behind in search of a lucrative and more comfortable life elsewhere in the galaxy.

canonicalStats.abilityText: All Whiphids receive a +4 bonus to their Strength, but suffer -2 penalties to both their Intelligence and Wisdom. Whiphids are typically strong, but many lack experience in the wider galaxy.

canonicalTraits.name: Cold Resistance
canonicalTraits.description: Their thick fur gives allows Whiphids to add a +5 Species bonus to their Fortitude Defense to resist Extreme Cold.

canonicalTraits.name: Natural Weapons
canonicalTraits.description: A Whiphid has Natural Weapons, in the form of sharp claws. When a Whiphid makes an Unarmed attack, it may choose to use its Natural Weapons, dealing 1d6 points of slashing damage with that attack, instead of normal Unarmed damage. Whiphid are always considered armed with their Natural Weapons.

canonicalTraits.name: Scent
canonicalTraits.description: Whiphids have a keen sense of smell. At close range (within 10 squares), Whiphids ignore Concealment and Cover for purposes of Perception checks, and take no penalty from poor visibility while Tracking.

name: Wookiee

structuralTraits.name: Mighty
structuralTraits.description: A Wookiee gains a +4 species bonus to Strength.

structuralTraits.name: Endurance
structuralTraits.description: A Wookiee gains a +2 species bonus to Fortitude Defense.

conditionalTraits.name: Rage
conditionalTraits.description: When reduced to half hit points or fewer, a Wookiee gains a +2 bonus on melee attack rolls and damage rolls until the end of the encounter.

description: Wookiees are widely recognized as one of the strongest and fiercest intelligent Species in the galaxy. Wookiees have many customs and traditions that revolve around honor and loyalty, including the special bond called the honor family and the sacred pledge called the Life Debt. A Wookiee never uses his or her climbing claws in combat; doing this is considered dishonorable and a sign of madness.

canonicalStats.abilityText: All Wookiees receive a +4 bonus to their Strength, and +2 bonus to their Constitution, but suffer -2 penalties to their Dexterity, Wisdom, and Charisma. Wookiees are powerful and hardy, but they aren't agile, tend to be impulsive, and have little patience for diplomacy.

canonicalTraits.name: Expert Climber
canonicalTraits.description: Wookiees are great climbers and may choose to Take 10 on Climb checks, even when distracted or threatened.

canonicalTraits.name: Extraordinary Recuperation
canonicalTraits.description: A Wookiee regains Hit Points at double the normal rate (see Natural Healing).

canonicalTraits.name: Intimidating Presence
canonicalTraits.description: Known to pull off the ears of Gundarks, Wookiees may choose to reroll any Persuasion check made to Intimidate others, but the result of the reroll must be accepted, even if it is worse.

canonicalTraits.name: Rage
canonicalTraits.description: Once per day, a Wookiee can fly into a Rage as a Swift Action. While Raging, the Wookiee temporarily gains a +2 Rage bonus on melee attack rolls and melee damage rolls, but cannot use Skills that require patience and concentration, such as Mechanics, Stealth, or Use the Force. A fit of Rage lasts for a number of rounds equal to 5 + the Wookiee's Constitution modifier. At the end of its Rage, the Wookiee moves -1 Persistent step along the Condition Track. The penalties imposed by this Persistent Condition persist until the Wookiee takes at least 10 minutes to recuperate, during which time the Wookiee can't engage in any strenuous activity.

canonicalTraits.name: Weapon Familiarity (Bowcaster and Ryyk Blade)
canonicalTraits.description: Wookiees treat the Bowcaster as a Rifle instead of an Exotic Weapon (Ranged). They additionally treat the Ryyk Blade as an Advanced Melee Weapon instead of an Exotic Weapon (Melee).

name: Wroonian

structuralTraits.name: Elongated Reach
structuralTraits.description: A Wroonian gains a +2 species bonus on grapple checks.

description: Wroonians are a blue-skinned Species of Near-Humans from the planet Wroona in The Inner Rim, a world in the same sector as the Bestine System. Wroonian society is wealthy, fun-loving, and deeply spiritual; as a result, Wroonians often wear ornate clothing and tattoos. Wroonians love travel both at sea and in space, often fancying themselves as skilled navigators and explorers. A well-traveled Species, they were represented in The Galactic Republic by the blue-skinned Pantoran Senator Chi Eekway Papanoida and later became part of The Galactic Empire. There are numerous Wroonian colonies spread throughout the Mid and Outer Rim, such as on Saleucami.

canonicalStats.abilityText: All Wroonians receive a +2 bonus to their Dexterity, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Constitution. Wroonians are graceful and swift, but often their physical prowess lags behind other Species.

canonicalTraits.name: Iron Will
canonicalTraits.description: Wroonians are stubborn and hard to be deterred, giving them a +2 Species bonus to their Will Defense.

canonicalTraits.name: Soothing Voice
canonicalTraits.description: A Wroonian may choose to reroll any Persuasion check, but the result of the reroll must be accepted, even if it is worse.

name: Yarkora

structuralTraits.name: Brute Force
structuralTraits.description: A Yarkora gains a +2 species bonus on melee damage rolls.

description: Yarkora are adept at gathering information, aided by their acute senses of sight and hearing. Most Yarkora try to keep a low profile by acting alone. However, those who find a ship or gang useful have no trouble gathering the needed henchmen, thanks to their natural charisma.

canonicalStats.abilityText: All Yarkora receive +2 bonuses to both their Wisdom and Charisma, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Dexterity. Yarkora are charismatic and street smart.

canonicalTraits.name: Bonus Class Skill
canonicalTraits.description: Yarkora are adept at collecting information from a variety of sources. Gather Information is always a Class Skill for Yarkora.

canonicalTraits.name: Confusion
canonicalTraits.description: Once per encounter as a Standard Action, a Yarkora can make a Deception check against the Will Defense of all targets within 6 squares. If the Deception check result equals or exceeds a target's Will Defense, that target does not Threaten any squares until the end of the Yarkora's next turn. This is a Mind-Affecting effect.

canonicalTraits.name: Deceptive
canonicalTraits.description: Naturally gifted at manipulation, a Yarkora can choose to reroll any Deception check, but the result of the reroll must be accepted, even if it is worse.

name: Yevetha

structuralTraits.name: Militaristic Culture
structuralTraits.description: A Yevetha gains a +2 species bonus on Initiative checks.

description: A tall, skeletal Species with lithe builds, the Yevetha have little impact on the galaxy until after the fall of the Empire, but even that slight influence makes them known for their intense xenophobia and a culture heavily centered upon death. During Emperor Palpatine's reign, the Yevetha's homeworld, N'zoth, is seized and made the site of an Imperial shipyard. When the Yevetha revolt against their Imperial masters shortly after the Battle of Yavin, they seize control of the shipyards and over the following decade proceed to refine their technological capabilities, devising new Starships and Weapons that enable them to seize and forcibly colonize over a dozen worlds in the Koornacht Cluster. They then embark on an ambitious campaign against the New Republic military, suffering a crushing defeat because of the influence of members of The Fallanassi sect of Force-users- a defeat that leaves many Yevetha dead because of their inborn refusal to accept surrender. The New Republic keeps the Species under watch until active patrols are removed close to a decade after the bloody battle, during which time the Yevetha build an even larger force than their original fleet.

canonicalStats.abilityText: All Yevetha receive a +2 bonus to their Strength, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Charisma. Yevetha are strong despite their gaunt physiques, but their fatalistic culture and violent xenophobia color their outlook.

canonicalTraits.name: Mechanical Aptitude
canonicalTraits.description: Yevetha have a knack for technology. Once per encounter, a Yevetha can treat any single Mechanics check as if the die roll is a Natural 20.

canonicalTraits.name: Natural Weapons
canonicalTraits.description: A Yevetha's retractable claws are Natural Weapons. When a Yevetha makes an Unarmed attack, it may choose to use its Natural Weapons, dealing 1d6 points of Slashing damage with the attack instead of normal Unarmed damage. A Yevetha is always considered armed with their Natural Weapons.

canonicalTraits.name: Xenophobia
canonicalTraits.description: Yevetha have an intense distrust of all Species other than their own, and gain a +2 Species bonus to their Will Defense against Persuasion checks made to improve their Attitude by any creature of a different Species.

name: Yuuzhan Vong

structuralTraits.name: Force Immunity
structuralTraits.description: A Yuuzhan Vong is immune to the effects of Force powers.

structuralTraits.name: Living Technology
structuralTraits.description: A Yuuzhan Vong may only use organic equipment.

description: The Yuuzhan Vong come from the unknown reaches of space, entering the galaxy as invaders fueled by religious conviction. They have no connection to The Force, and regard mechanical technology as blasphemous. Their Starships, weapons, armor, and tools are genetically engineered lifeforms or created from the byproducts of such creatures. After laying waste to many New Republic worlds, the invading Yuuzhan Vong are eventually overcome by The Galactic Alliance. Peace is restored, even though some Yuuzhan Vong remain behind in isolated corners of the galaxy.

variants.label: Alternate
variants.description: Alternate Yuuzhan Vong trait profile from the canonical species text.

traits.name: Force Immunity
traits.description: Yuuzhan Vong can't take the Force Sensitivity feat, can't make Use the Force checks, and never gain Force Points. In addition, they are immune to any Force effect that targets their Will Defense (including Force Powers and aspects of the Use the Force skill).

traits.name: Technophobic
traits.description: Yuuzhan Vong take a -5 penalty on attack rolls and skill checks made when using mechanical weapons or tools.

traits.name: Weapon Familiarity
traits.description: Yuuzhan Vong with the Weapon Proficiency (Simple Weapons) feat are considered proficient with the Amphistaff.

canonicalStats.abilityText: Yuuzhan Vong receive a +2 bonus to Strength, but suffer a -2 penalty to Wisdom. Yuuzhan Vong are powerfully built, but rash and quick to pass judgment.

canonicalTraits.name: Biotech Proficiency
canonicalTraits.description: Yuuzhan Vong suffer no penalties while wielding Yuuzhan Vong Biotech.

canonicalTraits.name: Force Immunity
canonicalTraits.description: Yuuzhan Vong can't take the Force Sensitivity feat, can't make Use the Force checks, and never gain Force Points. In addition, they are immune to any Force effect that targets their Will Defense (including Force Powers and aspects of the Use the Force skill).

canonicalTraits.name: Primitive
canonicalTraits.description: Yuuzhan Vong do not gain Weapon Proficiency (Heavy Weapons), Weapon Proficiency (Pistols), or Weapon Proficiency (Rifles), as starting Feats at 1st level, even if their Class normally grants them.

canonicalTraits.name: Weapon Familiarity
canonicalTraits.description: Yuuzhan Vong treat Amphistaffs as a Simple Weapon (Melee) instead of as an Exotic Weapons (Melee).

name: Yuzzem

structuralTraits.name: Expert Climber
structuralTraits.description: A Yuzzem can reroll Climb checks, but the result of the reroll must be accepted, even if it is worse.

structuralTraits.name: Natural Weapons
structuralTraits.description: A Yuzzem's large tusks and sharp claws serve as Natural Weapons. When making an Unarmed attack, a Yuzzem can use Natural Weapons, dealing 1d8 points of piercing damage (Bite), or slashing damage (Claws), instead of normal Unarmed damage. Yuzzem are always considered armed with their Natural Weapons.

structuralTraits.name: Physically Intimidating
structuralTraits.description: Yuzzem use their Strength modifier instead of their Charisma modifier for Persuasion checks made to Intimidate others.

structuralTraits.name: Scent
structuralTraits.description: At close range (within 10 squares), Yuzzem ignore Concealment and Cover for purposes of Perception checks, and they take no penalty from poor visibility when Tracking.

conditionalTraits.name: Rage
conditionalTraits.description: When reduced to half hit points or fewer, a Yuzzem gains a +2 species bonus on melee attack rolls until the end of the encounter.

description: In spite of a well-deserved reputation as temperamental brutes, Yuzzem are cunning and intelligent, and their resourcefulness gives them as much of an advantage as their physically powerful frames. The Empire has learned this the hard way after they declared the Yuzzem a slave Species, rounding up large numbers of them, and putting them to work in heavy-labor operations. The Yuzzem have frequently revolted against their Imperial masters, showing that they can be as ferocious as Wookiees, with no reservations about using their claws and tusks in a fight.

canonicalStats.abilityText: All Yuzzem receive a +4 bonus to their Strength, but suffer -2 penalties to both their Wisdom and Charisma. Yuzzem are physically powerful, but their volatile nature earns them few allies.

canonicalTraits.name: Expert Climber
canonicalTraits.description: A Yuzzem can reroll Climb checks, but the result of the reroll must be accepted, even if it is worse.

canonicalTraits.name: Natural Weapons
canonicalTraits.description: A Yuzzem's large tusks and sharp claws serve as Natural Weapons. When making an Unarmed attack, a Yuzzem can use Natural Weapons, dealing 1d8 points of piercing damage (Bite), or slashing damage (Claws), instead of normal Unarmed damage. Yuzzem are always considered armed with their Natural Weapons.

canonicalTraits.name: Physically Intimidating
canonicalTraits.description: Yuzzem use their Strength modifier instead of their Charisma modifier for Persuasion checks made to Intimidate others.

canonicalTraits.name: Scent
canonicalTraits.description: At close range (within 10 squares), Yuzzem ignore Concealment and Cover for purposes of Perception checks, and they take no penalty from poor visibility when Tracking.

name: Zabrak

structuralTraits.name: Battle Hardened
structuralTraits.description: A Zabrak gains a +2 species bonus to Fortitude Defense.

activatedAbilities.name: Irrepressible
activatedAbilities.description: Once per encounter, a Zabrak may ignore the effects of being stunned or dazed for one turn.

description: The Zabrak are an early spacefaring race distinguished by patterns of vestigial horns on their heads. Zabrak hail from many worlds and have been spacefarers for so long that they define themselves and each other according to their colony of origin. The harshness of Iridonia, their homeworld, forged in the Zabrak an iron will to survive. Driven to escape their world, they sought to obtain the knowledge of space flight. When Duros scouts began exploring The Mid Rim, they encountered Zabrak in eight sovereign colonies in five different systems.

canonicalStats.abilityText: Zabrak have no Ability Score adjustments.

canonicalTraits.name: Heightened Awareness
canonicalTraits.description: Having strong survival instincts and quick reactions, a Zabrak may choose to reroll any Perception check, but the result of the reroll must be accepted, even if it is worse.

canonicalTraits.name: Superior Defenses
canonicalTraits.description: Adapted to a very tough and trying environment, Zabrak gain a +1 Species bonus to all their Defenses.

name: Zeltron

structuralTraits.name: Pheromones
structuralTraits.description: A Zeltron gains a +5 species bonus on Persuasion checks.

description: Zeltrons are known throughout the galaxy for their hedonistic nature. Experiencing pleasure in its myriad forms is the lifetime pursuit of all Zeltrons. They are a fun-loving species, placing recreation before other, less worldly interests. Members of the Species are found throughout the galaxy, for they enjoy traveling, meeting new people, indulging in their passions, and experiencing new things. Zeltrons are born with a latent empathy that allows them to sense the emotions of others.

canonicalStats.abilityText: Zeltrons receive a +2 bonus to their Charisma, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Wisdom. Zeltrons get along well with others, yet they are prone to fits of passion.

canonicalTraits.name: Empathy
canonicalTraits.description: Zeltrons add their Charisma bonus (if any) to Perception checks made to Sense Deception or Sense Influence.

canonicalTraits.name: Soothing Pheromones
canonicalTraits.description: A Zeltron gains a +5 Species bonus on Persuasion checks made to Change Attitude. This bonus does not apply to other Zeltrons, as Zeltrons are acclimated to each other's pheromones.

name: Zygerrian

structuralTraits.name: Dominating Presence
structuralTraits.description: A Zygerrian gains a +2 species bonus on Intimidate checks.

description: A violent Species from the distant corners of The Outer Rim, Zygerrians are known for their history of piracy and war. As a Species that earns its fame through slavery, piracy, and violence, Zygerrians are feared in spaceports throughout the galaxy. Raised in a culture where weakness can mean death or enslavement, Zygerrians cultivate a dangerous, unpredictable image to deter others from challenging their dominance. Those who do must contend with the Zygerrians' natural combat prowess, since Zygerrians are excellent fighters with or without weapons. Zygerrian society is built on the principle that strength leads to authority, and for Zygerrians, strength can mean many things- physical strength, and also strength of will, or conviction, and of bravery.

canonicalStats.abilityText: All Zygerrians receive a +2 bonus to their Constitution, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Wisdom. Zygerrians are durable, but are considered reckless by other Species.

canonicalTraits.name: Bonus Feat
canonicalTraits.description: Zygerrians receive the Martial Arts I feat at 1st level as a bonus Feat.

canonicalTraits.name: Iron Will
canonicalTraits.description: Zygerrians have above-average willpower, gaining a +2 Species bonus to their Will Defense.

canonicalTraits.name: Strength of Conviction
canonicalTraits.description: Zygerrians can use their Charisma modifier in place of their Wisdom modifier when calculating their Will Defense.
